pso.name: Basketball Québec (Fédération de basketball du Québec)
pso.software: Website/CMS: Spordle (Publication Sports platform, 'Powered by Spordle' footer). League scheduling/standings: Kreezee Sports (circuit-basketball-quebec.kreezee-sports.com and ligue-basketball-quebec.kreezee-sports.com). Event/festival registration: EventNroll (app.eventnroll.com). 3x3 events: FIBA play.fiba3x3.com.
pso.notes: Founded 1970, 60,000+ members province-wide (basketball.qc.ca/fr/page/a_propos/qui_sommes-nous.html). DG: Daniel Grimard (dgrimard@basketball.qc.ca x3511); competitions (CBQ/LBQ): Yannick Mercier (ymercier@basketball.qc.ca x3696). Member-club registry = 'Où Jouer?' page with 17 regional PDF club lists hosted at publicationsports.com/ressources/files/737/<Region>.pdf (downloads require a Referer header; e.g. Montreal.xlsx.pdf, Rive-Sud.pdf, Lac_St-Louis.pdf all verified live 2026-07; lists mix civil clubs, community orgs and school programs so needs filtering for a club census). The PSO's youth-league directory page (fr/page/reseau_basketball_quebec/ligue_-_de_18_ans.html) officially lists only two U18 leagues: its own LBQ and the independent Montreal Basketball League; the elite pathway is the invitational Circuit Basketball Québec. IMPORTANT MARKET CONTEXT: in Quebec the dominant U14-U18 competitive structure is school-based via RSEQ (rseq.ca, benjamin/cadet/juvénile D1-D3 provincial leagues + championships) — excluded from this census per scope (school/varsity), which is why the civil club-league layer is thinner than Ontario's.

leagues.name: Circuit Basketball Québec (CBQ)
leagues.operator: Basketball Québec (PSO)
leagues.ages_tiers: Invitational elite circuit, boys & girls: Atome (b. Oct 2013–Sep 2014), Benjamin (b. Oct 2012–Sep 2013), Cadet majeur (b. Oct 2010–Sep 2012), Juvénile (b. Oct 2008–Sep 2010); teams from clubs/associations/sport-études programs
leagues.region: Quebec province-wide (teams from Montréal, Québec City, Estrie, etc.)
leagues.scale: Team count not published; invitation-only high-level teams from organizations across the province; 4 regular-season stages (2 games per stage) + playoffs; $1,850 per team + affiliation fees
leagues.season: Recruitment May–June; registration September; stages October–February; playoffs Feb 13–14, 2027 at Cégep Édouard-Montpetit (2026-27 season)
leagues.software: Kreezee Sports (circuit-basketball-quebec.kreezee-sports.com) for schedules/standings; PSO site on Spordle/Publication Sports
leagues.notes: The province's top civil (non-school) youth circuit; replaced older regional elite networks. Regional network pages for all 17 regions point clubs to CBQ + LBQ as the two PSO competitions.

leagues.name: Ligue Basketball Québec (LBQ)
leagues.operator: Basketball Québec (PSO)
leagues.ages_tiers: Novice (b. Oct 2016–Sep 2018) and Mini (b. Oct 2014–Sep 2016), boys & girls, levels A / AA / AAA; three sections: Montérégie, Rive-Nord/Montréal, Provinciale (Mini AAA only); max one team per organization per category
leagues.region: Greater Montreal (Montérégie + Rive-Nord/Montréal sections) with a province-wide Mini AAA section
leagues.scale: Team count not published; each team plays 10 games (6 in centralized days + 4 hybrid); fees $1,000 (Novice A/AA, Mini A) to $1,100 (Mini AA/AAA) per team
leagues.season: Registration Sept 1–30, 2026; regular season through Feb 28, 2027; playoffs Mar 13–14, 2027 at Cégep Édouard-Montpetit
leagues.software: Kreezee Sports (ligue-basketball-quebec.kreezee-sports.com) for schedules/standings
leagues.notes: PSO's entry-level competitive league for U10-U12; participating orgs seen on Kreezee include BMAA/NMAA Club de basketball de Montréal-Nord and other member clubs.

leagues.name: Montreal Basketball League (MBL) / Ligue de Basketball de Montréal
leagues.operator: Independent not-for-profit volunteer organization (president Joey McKitterick), recognized by Basketball Québec and listed in its official U18 league directory
leagues.ages_tiers: Ages 7–17: Novice, Mini, Bantam, Cadet, Juvénile; boys and girls; levels A / AA / AAA
leagues.region: Greater Montreal (island of Montreal, Laval, South Shore incl. Châteauguay and St-Jean-sur-Richelieu)
leagues.scale: ~20+ member clubs/organizations (Brookwood, Châteauguay Ravens, DC Blues, LaSalle Leaders, Laval Nobel, Monarques, Parc-Ex Knights, St-Jean Riverains, Sun Youth, Tornades, WILBA, etc.); dozens of teams across divisions
leagues.season: Fall–winter (e.g. 'Fall 2025-26'), regular season plus March playoffs ('March Madness')
leagues.software: Spordle / Publication Sports (basketballmontreal.org 'Powered by Spordle'; by-laws and files hosted on media.publicationsports.com)
leagues.notes: THE Montreal metro minor-basketball league that most civil clubs' AA/AAA teams play in. 2025-26 by-laws PDF at media.publicationsports.com/1061/1f0a9de8-a31d-62a6-a224-0242ac120003 (403 to non-browser fetches).

leagues.name: Festival d'Été Basketball Québec
leagues.operator: Basketball Québec (PSO)
leagues.ages_tiers: 12U, 13U, 14U boys & girls + Espoirs 13U; open to all skill levels; BQ affiliation mandatory (out-of-province teams accepted with insurance proof)
leagues.region: Province-wide, hosted in Longueuil (Centre sportif du Cégep Édouard-Montpetit)
leagues.scale: 80+ teams annually; $590 per team (12U/13U/14U), $490 (Espoirs)
leagues.season: July 3–5, 2026 (annual, registration deadline June 20)
leagues.software: EventNroll (app.eventnroll.com) for team registration
leagues.notes: The PSO's marquee summer club event (festival/jamboree format rather than a multi-week league); companion Festival d'Automne referenced historically but current page details only the summer edition.

leagues.name: Circuit Basketball Québec 3X3
leagues.operator: Basketball Québec (PSO)
leagues.ages_tiers: Youth edition (historical, ~2020-21): novice, mini, benjamin, cadet, juvénile + ouvert, for BQ-affiliated organizations. Summer 2025 relaunch targeted athletes born 2002–2007 (collegiate/university), i.e. NOT youth
leagues.region: Montreal (2025 stages: Auditorium de Verdun, École Antoine-de-Saint-Exupéry, McGill University)
leagues.scale: 2025 relaunch: 8 male + 8 female teams per stage, 3 stages; $300/team first stage, $150 additional stages
leagues.season: Summer (June); youth-category edition last documented 2020-21 (fall weekends)
leagues.software: FIBA play.fiba3x3.com for 2025 registration; EventNroll on the older youth-circuit page
leagues.notes: Included for completeness: the youth (novice–juvénile) 3x3 circuit page is 2020-vintage; the active 2025 circuit is 18+/collegiate. Whether a youth 3x3 circuit currently operates is unconfirmed.

leagues.name: Ligue de mini-basket de Gatineau (LMBG)
leagues.operator: Association de basketball de Gatineau (ABG) — community association, founded 1997
leagues.ages_tiers: Recreational league for boys & girls in elementary grades 1–6; ABG also fields the Dragons de Gatineau competitive teams U12–U17 (girls U12/U13/U14/U15/U16-17, boys U12–U17)
leagues.scale: ABG serves ~500 players, trainers and coaches per year
leagues.season: 3 sessions of 8 weeks each: fall, winter, spring; Dragons fall-winter season late August–late March
leagues.notes: Which league the Dragons' competitive teams play in is not stated on the site (Outaouais teams historically cross into Eastern Ontario competition; unverified). ABG is the anchor youth basketball org for the Outaouais region.

leagues.name: DBC Youth Basketball League (YBL)
leagues.operator: Dexter's Basketball Club Inc. (DBC), Montreal — club-run league, operating since 1996 (contact Dexter John, also runs DJ Sports Club in Brossard)
leagues.ages_tiers: Boys & girls ages 7–16
leagues.region: Montreal and surrounding areas (South Shore/Brossard, Sud-Ouest)
leagues.scale: Team/player counts not published; full-season format, games hosted Saturdays through the summer; medals and championship banners
leagues.season: Spring/summer (Saturday games); 'Registration Spring/Summer 2026 opened'
leagues.software: Qidigo for registration; LeagueLineup.com for schedules/rules
leagues.notes: Self-described as one of the most affordable youth leagues in Montreal; DBC also runs a youth fall-winter season, summer camps and adult programs. Dexter's Basketball Club appears in Basketball Québec's Montréal and Rive-Sud regional club registries.

leagues.name: Ligue Jeunesse Rebels (LBRRNJ) / Mini-Rebels — Rebels Rive-Nord
leagues.operator: Rebels Rive-Nord (Ligue de Basketball Rive-Nord Montréal) — private club-run league, 10+ years operating
leagues.ages_tiers: Youth division ages 5–15 (Mini-Rebels + parent-child programs); the organization's main league is adult, multi-division
leagues.scale: Organization-wide: 'hundreds of active members' and 'dozens of teams' per season (youth-only split not published)
leagues.season: Year-round sessions; also selection camps and summer day camps
leagues.software: Matchflow (app.matchflow.ca/rebels-basketball) for registration/league management
leagues.notes: Self-described 'largest basketball league on the Rive-Nord'; primarily adult with a growing youth arm — flagging mainly for the Matchflow platform intel and Rive-Nord coverage.

leagues.name: Basketball St-Laurent Ligue Maison (house league)
leagues.operator: Basketball St-Laurent — community club (Saint-Laurent borough, Montreal)
leagues.ages_tiers: Recreational house league ages 5–17 (weekly Saturday play); also Interurbain/Intercity competitive teams (tryouts, $425 + $50 uniform deposit) and an adapted program ages 5–25
leagues.scale: Player counts not published; house league + intercity teams + adapted program out of Complexe Sportif St-Laurent
leagues.season: September–April (house league and intercity); summer workshops/rec sessions
leagues.software: Kaypic (register.kaypic.com/slbasket) for all registrations
leagues.notes: Listed in Basketball Québec's Montréal regional registry (Comité de basketball Saint-Laurent). Kaypic is a lesser-known Quebec registration platform — competitive intel. Intercity competitive teams most likely play in the MBL (unconfirmed).

leagues.name: Ligue maison Nobel — Association Basketball Laval (ABL)
leagues.operator: Association Basketball Laval (ABL) — community association
leagues.ages_tiers: Basket pour tous: Pré-novice (5–7), Novice (8–9), Mini (10–11); Ligue maison Nobel for ages 12–13 and 14–16; Nobel Elite competitive teams from Mini (10–11) to Juvénile (16–17) representing Laval in regional and provincial competition
leagues.scale: Counts not published; 12-week sessions, $200 (younger) / $250 (12–16) + $50 uniform/ball
leagues.season: Fall and winter 12-week sessions (school-year); elite teams fall–winter
leagues.software: Amilia for online registration
leagues.notes: ABL's 'Laval Nobel' elite teams appear as a member club of the Montreal Basketball League; ABL is the anchor association in BQ's Laval region.

leagues.name: Miller Basketball Academy leagues (Laval)
leagues.operator: Miller Basketball Academy — private academy
leagues.ages_tiers: Academy-run youth leagues by category, e.g. Ligue Juvénile garçons (boys U17-18); also training programs
leagues.software: Shopify storefront on own domain for league/program registration (millerbasketballacademy.ca/products/...)
leagues.notes: Private-academy in-house league model (pay-to-play via e-commerce checkout rather than a league-management platform). Scale and season windows not published; single-source.

leagues.name: Ligues de matchs EMBQ (École de mini-basketball de Québec)
leagues.operator: École de mini-basketball de Québec (EMBQ) — private mini-basketball school / community org
leagues.ages_tiers: Mini-basketball courses ages 4–12 with game leagues (8 games per league session); also sport-études feeder (sec. 1–5) and adult leagues (excluded)
leagues.region: Quebec City (multiple school and municipal gyms)
leagues.season: School-year sessions (weekend courses + leagues); winter league documented
leagues.notes: Listed in Basketball Québec's Québec/Chaudière-Appalaches regional registry ('L'École de Mini-Basketball de Québec', Lac-Beauport). League-page URL is 2019-dated though the organization is active — current league volume unverified. Quebec City's competitive youth teams otherwise play via CBQ and school (RSEQ) structures.

clubs.name: Atelier 803 (formerly L'Académie de basketball de Thetford Mines; teams brand: Team Thetford Basketball)
clubs.principal: Igor Rwigema — founder (2012 in Alma, Thetford Mines since 2014); Armel Mampouya — director
clubs.programs: Boarding basketball academy, ~30-50 student-athletes/yr (secondary + cégep, from Québec, rest of Canada, Caribbean, Europe); 3 civil teams (Team Thetford) playing elite prep circuits in the US and Ontario; academics mandatory; summer camps; alumni include Chris Boucher (NBA) and Quincy Guerrier
leagues: US/Ontario elite prep circuits (unspecified, outside RSEQ)
clubs.team_league_map: Team Thetford's 3 civil teams → US and Ontario elite circuits (Le Soleil, 2020)
clubs.notes: Highest-profile private basketball academy in eastern Quebec

clubs.name: Énergie Basketball (incl. Impact Basketball, Shawinigan Prep, Volts Basketball Féminin)

leagues: Circuit Basketball Québec (CBQ) — 'circuit civil de Basketball Québec'
clubs.team_league_map: Cadet and juvénile teams entered the CBQ civil circuit at launch (Le Nouvelliste, Nov 2020); players must also play for their school program
clubs.notes: Hub of the Mondou/Gym Impact basketball ecosystem in Shawinigan (Énergie + Impact Academy + Shawinigan Prep) | Also listed as: Énergie Basketball

clubs.name: Club de Basketball Saint-Hyacinthe (STH) / V-Kings de Saint-Hyacinthe (formerly Vikings)
clubs.address: Registrations at Centre sportif de l'école Saint-Joseph, 2850 avenue de la Bruère, Saint-Hyacinthe, QC

clubs.notes: Vikings (old PSO PDF), V-Kings (press/site) and STH (current BQ registry) share Patrick Kervin's phone 514-885-4347 — treated as one evolving organization. v-kingsbasketball.ca did not resolve at fetch time; alternate phone in press: 514-892-8278. | Also listed as: Club de Basketball Saint-Hyacinthe (STH) / V-Kings de Saint-Hyacinthe

Older Wix site: basketballstjean.wixsite.com/monsite. | TYPE: community nonprofit rep association (association personnifiée, NEQ 1164366149, registered 2007-04-16, status immatriculée). Board per REQ mirror: Eric Gagnon (président), Josée Latulippe (trésorière), Eric Faucher (secrétaire); administrators Steeve Gauvin, Jérome Dupuis, Steve Paquin, Sylvie Trahan, Elaine Beaudry, Rostand Phaneuf, Pierre Boivin. UNVERIFIED LEAD: search snippets of the SJSR city directory list a responsable 'Simon Bravo, (514) 770-1565, simon.rbm@gmail.com' (Bravo also coaches at Cégep Saint-Jean-sur-Richelieu) but this did not render on the fetched page — treat as lead, not confirmed. Teams 'Les Riverains' compete in Basketball Montréal league (AAA/AA/A + girls). Tryout camps at Polyvalente Chanoine-Armand-Racicot. Old secondary site on Wix (basketballstjean.wixsite.com/monsite). Socials: facebook.com/asso.basket.sj.rivs, instagram.com/lesriverains. SOURCES: https://organismes.sjsr.ca/item/association-de-basketball-de-saint-jean-sur-richelieu/ ; http://www.quebecentreprises.com/association-de-basketball-de-saint-jean-sur-richelieu-8ayb/ ; https://www.basketballstjean.ca/association-basketball-st-jean ; https://www.basketballmontreal.org/en/page/teams/st_jean_sur_richelieu_les_riverains.html

verification.claim: Circuit Basketball Québec (CBQ) is operated by Basketball Québec (PSO); invitation-only high-level teams; 4 regular-season stages (2 games per stage) + playoffs; $1,850/team + affiliation fees; Kreezee Sports for schedules/standings; PSO site on Spordle/Publication Sports
verification.evidence: Basketball Québec's own CBQ presentation page (https://www.basketball.qc.ca/en/page/circuit_basketball_quebec_presentation.html) confirms every element: 'an invitational circuit, which brings together high-level teams from organizations, associations, sport-study programs or concentrations'; 4 stages (2026-10-17/18, 11-14, 12-12, 2027-01-16) each comprising two games of four 8-minute periods; playoffs Feb 13-14, 2027 at Cégep Édouard-Montpetit; registration fee $1,850.00/team which 'does not include the 2026-2027 affiliation fees for the organization, coaches, and athletes'; no team count published. Kreezee independently corroborated: live site https://circuit-basketball-quebec.kreezee-sports.com/ plus the PSO's CBQ hub page (https://www.basketball.qc.ca/fr/page/index/circuit_basketball_quebec.html) linking 'Horaires et classements' to circuit-basketball-quebec.kreezee-sports.com/calendrier. PSO on Publication Sports/Spordle corroborated: BQ's official club directory PDFs are served from www.publicationsports.com (e.g., https://www.publicationsports.com/ressources/files/737/Quebec-Chaudiere_appalaches.pdf). Extra competitive intel: an Uplifter help-center article exists titled 'Basketball Québec - Registration process for the CBQ' (https://learn.uplifterinc.com/hc/en-us/articles/42491672135955), indicating team/member registration flows through Uplifter while Kreezee handles schedules/standings — worth adding to the platform column.

verification.claim: Ligue Basketball Québec (LBQ) is operated by Basketball Québec; team count not published; 10 games per team (6 centralized + 4 hybrid); fees $1,000 (Novice A/AA, Mini A) to $1,100 (Mini AA/AAA); Kreezee Sports at ligue-basketball-quebec.kreezee-sports.com
verification.evidence: PSO page https://www.basketball.qc.ca/fr/page/ligue_basketball_quebec.html confirms fees exactly as claimed: Novice A $1,000, Novice AA $1,000, Mini A $1,000, Mini AA $1,100, Mini AAA $1,100; 10 matches total (6 in centralized days organized by Basketball Québec + 4 hybrid per team availability); administered by Basketball Québec; three sections (Montérégie, Rive-Nord/Montréal, Provinciale Mini AAA); schedules/standings at ligue-basketball-quebec.kreezee-sports.com/calendrier. Independently corroborated by the live Kreezee site itself (https://ligue-basketball-quebec.kreezee-sports.com/ with calendar and team pages, e.g. /equipes/club-de-basket-revolution-des-laurentides-125653). Minor nuance, not a contradiction: BQ publishes a CAP of 11 teams max per category (one per organization), though no actual team count.

verification.claim: Montreal Basketball League (MBL) / Ligue de Basketball de Montréal is an independent not-for-profit volunteer organization (president Joey McKitterick), recognized by Basketball Québec and listed in its official U18 league directory; ~20+ member clubs (Brookwood, Châteauguay Ravens, DC Blues, LaSalle Leaders, Laval Nobel, Monarques, Parc-Ex Knights, St-Jean Riverains, Sun Youth, Tornades, WILBA, etc.); Spordle/Publication Sports platform
verification.evidence: Independently corroborated by Basketball Québec's official U18 league directory (https://www.basketball.qc.ca/fr/page/reseau_basketball_quebec/ligue_-_de_18_ans.html), which lists 'Montreal Basketball League (MBL)', Montréal et environs, categories novice à juvénile AA-AAA — one of only two leagues in that directory. League site (https://www.basketballmontreal.org/en/contact.html) names Joey McKitterick as President (montrealbasketball@gmail.com, 514-823-2921); homepage describes a community-based volunteer organization recognized by the Quebec basketball federation. Organization page (http://www.basketballmontreal.org/en/page/organization.html) lists member clubs matching the claim: Brookwood, Chateauguay Ravens, DBC Hawks/Bobcats, DC Blues, LaSalle Leaders, Laval Nobel, Monarques, Page, Parc Ex Knights, St-Jean Riverains, Sun Youth Hornets, Tornades, WILBA and others across AAA/A+AA/Girls divisions, with a verified 'Powered by Spordle' footer. Not-for-profit status is self-described (no registry entry located), but nothing contradicts it; '~20+' member organizations is consistent with the 14+ named clubs plus others across divisions.

verification.claim: Festival d'Été Basketball Québec is operated by Basketball Québec; 80+ teams annually; $590/team (12U/13U/14U), $490 (Espoirs); EventNroll (app.eventnroll.com) for team registration
verification.evidence: PSO Festival pages (https://www.basketball.qc.ca/fr/page/evenments/festival_dautomne_et_dete.html and /en/page/event/summer_festival.html) confirm BQ operates it (2026 edition July 3-5 at Cégep Édouard-Montpetit, BQ-affiliated teams only) with fees exactly as claimed: $590 for 12U/13U/14U M+F, $490 for Espoirs 13U. EventNroll confirmed via live registration/ticketing page https://app.eventnroll.com/fr/billetterie/achat-de-billet/1159/9682 ('FESTIVAL D'ÉTÉ'). Scale corroborated by BQ's 36th-edition news item (https://www.basketball.qc.ca/fr/publication/nouvelle/36eme_edition_du_festival_dete.html): 'près de 120 équipes' and 1,000+ players — supports '80+ teams annually' as a safe floor. Extra intel: schedules also appear on a Kreezee subdomain (https://festival-ete-basketball-quebec.kreezee-sports.com/).

verification.claim: Circuit Basketball Québec 3X3 is operated by Basketball Québec; 2025 relaunch with 8 male + 8 female teams per stage, 3 stages; $300/team first stage, $150 additional; FIBA play.fiba3x3.com for 2025 registration; EventNroll on the older youth-circuit page
verification.evidence: BQ's launch announcement (https://www.basketball.qc.ca/fr/publication/nouvelle/basketball_quebec_lance_son_circuit_3x3.html, EN version /en/publication/news/basketball_quebec_announce_the_launch_of_the_basketball_quebec_3x3_circuit_for_summer_2025.html) confirms: '8 équipes masculines et 8 équipes féminines par étape'; three events (Stage 1 June 7 Verdun Auditorium, Stage 2 June 14 École Antoine-de-Saint-Exupéry, Final June 28 McGill; final entry free); $300/team first stage, $150 (50% off) second stage; registration through the official FIBA portal play.fiba3x3.com; winners qualify for Sun Life 3X3 Canada Hoops Finals in Toronto. The older page (https://www.basketball.qc.ca/fr/page/evenments/circuit_basketball_quebec_3x3.html) carries an EventNroll (app.eventnroll.com) registration link with Fall 2020-era documents, exactly as claimed.

verification.claim: Ligue de mini-basket de Gatineau (LMBG) is operated by Association de basketball de Gatineau (ABG), a community association founded 1997; ABG serves ~500 players, trainers and coaches per year; software unknown
verification.evidence: ABG's site (https://www.basketballgatineau.com/) states founding in 1997, non-profit status, ~100 volunteers, and services to ~500 players/coaches/instructors per year; dedicated league pages exist ('Ligue de basketball de Gatineau – Mini-basket' at https://www.basketballgatineau.com/abg/rec/lbg-mini/ and LMBG news category at /category/nouvelles/rec/lmbg/ — grades 1-6, three 8-week sessions per season). Existence independently corroborated by the Ville de Gatineau's Ado Gatineau community directory entry (https://www.gatineau.ca/portail/default.aspx?p=ado_gatineau/details&id=2363, mission across Aylmer/Hull/Gatineau sectors) and their active Facebook page posting LMBG match schedules (https://www.facebook.com/bbgatineau/). No third-party league-management platform observed (WordPress site) — software '?' stands.

verification.claim: DBC Youth Basketball League (YBL) is operated by Dexter's Basketball Club Inc. (DBC), Montreal, operating since 1996 (contact Dexter John, also runs DJ Sports Club in Brossard); full-season format, Saturday games through the summer, medals and championship banners; Qidigo for registration, LeagueLineup.com for schedules/rules
verification.evidence: DBC's YBL page (https://www.dbcmtl.org/ybl) confirms: games 'played bi-weekly in a full-season format', 'all games are hosted on Saturdays throughout the summer program', boys and girls ages 7-16 competing 'for medals and championship banners', founded 1996 ('Since 1996' also on https://www.dbcmtl.org/). Registration links point to Qidigo (qidigo.com/u/DBC-Montreal/activities/session) and schedules are hosted on LeagueLineup — both platforms verified on the page. Dexter John / DJ Sports Club Brossard corroborated independently: https://www.djscmtl.org/ (DJ Sports Club, leadership Dexter John) and the LeagueLineup site https://www.leaguelineup.com/welcome.asp?url=dbcbasketballmtl titled 'DJ Sports Club Inc. - (Brossard, QC)'. ZoomInfo lists 'Dexter's Basketball Club Inc' as the corporate entity (https://www.zoominfo.com/c/dexters-basketball-club-inc/368501568).

verification.claim: Ligue Jeunesse Rebels (LBRRNJ) / Mini-Rebels is operated by Rebels Rive-Nord (Ligue de Basketball Rive-Nord Montréal), private club-run league operating 10+ years; 'hundreds of active members' and 'dozens of teams' per season org-wide (youth split not published); Matchflow (app.matchflow.ca/rebels-basketball) for registration/league management
verification.evidence: https://rebelsrivenord.com/ (Blainville — note: distinct from the similarly named 'Rebelles Rive-Nord' club in Laval at rebellesrivenord.com) states 'Depuis plus de 10 ans', 'des centaines de membres actifs chaque saison', 'des dizaines d'équipes', and lists youth programming including Mini-Rebels (ages 5-15) and 'Ligue Jeunesse Rebels (LBRRNJ) — Ligue de basketball jeunesse rive-nord', with navigation links for registration/schedules/standings/rosters pointing to app.matchflow.ca. The Matchflow instance is live at https://app.matchflow.ca/rebels-basketball (page header 'Rebels Basketball | Ligue de basketball adulte' — the landing view fronts the adult league, but the club site routes youth registrations through the same Matchflow platform). Existence corroborated by their Facebook page (https://www.facebook.com/rebellesrivenord/, Blainville QC). Scale figures are the org's own published wording, as the claim itself notes.

verification.claim: Club École de mini-basketball de Québec (EMBQ), Québec City (HQ Lac-Beauport), exists and its teams play in its own house-league match circuit (Ligues de matchs EMBQ)
verification.evidence: Club site live at https://embq.qc.ca/ with HQ at 3 Chemin de la Coulée, Lac-Beauport G3B 1G5 (https://embq.qc.ca/contact/, 581-741-6658, embq@embq.qc.ca). Dedicated 'Ligues de matchs' page (https://embq.qc.ca/ligue-embq-hiver-2019/) confirms an in-house match league requiring enrollment in EMBQ's parascolaire/extrascolaire programs. Independently corroborated twice by the PSO: Basketball Québec's official Québec/Chaudière-Appalaches club-directory PDF (https://www.publicationsports.com/ressources/files/737/Quebec-Chaudiere_appalaches.pdf — 'École de mini basketball de Québec (EMBQ)', Québec, contact Mélanie Gauhtier [sic], 'de petit basket à adulte') and BQ's regional page (https://www.basketball.qc.ca/fr/page/reseau_basketball_quebec/quebecchaudiere-appalaches.html — EMBQ, Lac-Beauport, Mélanie Gauthier, admin@embq.qc.ca, 418-805-3145). Platform intel: registrations run on ProInscription (https://embq.proinscription.com/).

verification.claim: Club Institut de développement sportif de Québec (Lac-Beauport) exists; league play unknown
verification.evidence: Listed on Basketball Québec's official Québec/Chaudière-Appalaches regional page (https://www.basketball.qc.ca/fr/page/reseau_basketball_quebec/quebecchaudiere-appalaches.html): 'Institut de développement sportif de Québec', Lac-Beauport, contact Melanie Gauthier (melgauthier07@gmail.com, phone printed 418-8053-145, i.e. 418-805-3145). Caveats for the pipeline: this PSO listing is the ONLY footprint found — general web and registry searches surface nothing else under this name (searches return only the unrelated Institut national du sport du Québec) — and its contact person and phone number are identical to EMBQ's (same Lac-Beauport base), so it is very likely an EMBQ-affiliated/sister entity rather than an independent operating club. League play unknown stands.

verification.claim: Club Hope Basketball Québec (École Hope Basketball Québec Inc.), Québec City, exists; league play unknown
verification.correction: Club existence is solid, but the corporate name needs correcting: the Quebec enterprise register (NEQ 1174875816, via https://opengovca.com/quebec-business/1174875816) shows the entity was incorporated 2019-09-23 as 'Académie Hope Basketball Québec Inc.' (not 'École...'), with current legal name '9531-9000 Québec inc.' (since 2024-12-27) operating under the name 'Hope Basketball Québec' (activity code 8541 'Académie de Basket'). 'École Hope Basketball Québec Inc.' appears only in Basketball Québec's regional directory listing and matches no registry name. Type: private academy.
verification.evidence: Multiple independent sources confirm the club: Basketball Québec regional page (https://www.basketball.qc.ca/fr/page/reseau_basketball_quebec/quebecchaudiere-appalaches.html) lists 'École Hope Basketball Québec Inc.', Québec, contact Christian Savard, info.hopebasketballquebec@gmail.com, 418-998-2223; Quebec enterprise register via opengovca (NEQ 1174875816, addresses 336 rue de la Parmentière / 65 rue Durand, Québec); active Facebook page (https://www.facebook.com/hopebbqc/) with camps at Séminaire des Pères Maristes; registration on ProInscription (https://hopebasketballquebec.proinscription.com/); fields teams in BQ's Festival d'Été (team page on https://festival-ete-basketball-quebec.kreezee-sports.com/equipes/hope-basketball-132144) and runs the 'Challenge Hope Basketball - Rouge et Or' event at Université Laval (https://www.basketball.qc.ca/en/event/34687.html).

verification.claim: Club VOLT Basketball (Québec City) exists; league play unknown
verification.evidence: Two separate PSO data points: (1) Basketball Québec's Québec/Chaudière-Appalaches regional page (https://www.basketball.qc.ca/fr/page/reseau_basketball_quebec/quebecchaudiere-appalaches.html) lists 'VOLT Basketball', Québec, contact Guillaume L'Ecuyer, glecuyercourtier@gmail.com, 418-806-5899; (2) BQ's event calendar carries a sanctioned 'Tournoi des VOLTS basketball' scheduled July 10-12, 2026 (https://www.basketball.qc.ca/en/event/35082.html), indicating an operating organization, not just a paper listing. No standalone club website found; league play unknown stands.

pso.name: Basketball BC
pso.email: info@basketball.bc.ca (club membership: clubs@basketball.bc.ca; member services/registration: ssommerfeldt@basketball.bc.ca)
pso.software: RAMP Registrations (membership + officials registration at bbcoc.rampregistrations.com; site provides a 'Ramp Registration System' guide)
pso.notes: 40 member clubs listed for 2025-26 across 8 regions (Fraser Valley, Fraser River, Vancouver-Coastal, Thompson-Okanagan, Kootenays, Vancouver Island-Central Coast, Cariboo North-East, North West); prior-year registry at https://basketball.bc.ca/clubs/member-clubs-2024-25/. Clubs join via annual Club Membership Application + per-member data/fees. Key point for GTM: Basketball BC does NOT operate a provincial youth club league — it runs camps (NEXGEN, IGNITE), Team BC U15/U17 provincial teams, BC Summer Games (U14), and ADULT leagues; the youth club-league space is filled by independent operators (BCCBA/BC Hoops, community associations, private academies). Hybrid office, no regular office hours.

verification.claim: BCCBA / BC Hoops (BC Club Basketball Association) — operated by BCCBA, contact David Munro; founded as collective of clubs incl. 3D Basketball, AthElite, BC Bounce, Split Second, VanCity Basketball; scale 24 (2021) -> 102 (2022) -> 175 (2023) -> 196 teams (2024) per own site; fastest-growing club league in BC; software unknown
verification.evidence: Own site https://bccbaleague.com/ verified verbatim: contact David Munro (davidjaymunro@gmail.com), team counts 24/102/175/196 for 2021-2024, self-claim 'BC's fastest growing youth Basketball League' (note: site wording is 'youth Basketball League', not 'club league'; growth figures remain self-published only). INDEPENDENT corroboration of the collective-of-clubs founding: member club BC Bounce's own site https://pat-lee.squarespace.com/boys states '3D Basketball, AthElite, BC Bounce, Split Second and VanCity Basketball are coming together for a winter championship league' as programs 'forming the BC Club Basketball Association'. Member clubs AthElite Youth Basketball Association (S0077047) and 3D Youth Basketball Association (S0080568) are ACTIVE registered BC societies per OrgBook BC (https://orgbook.gov.bc.ca). No registration-software evidence found on bccbaleague.com — software stays unknown. Side note: no 'BC Club Basketball Association' entity in OrgBook; an ACTIVE 'Greater Vancouver Club Basketball Association' society (S0078808) exists and may be the related legal entity.

verification.claim: V&DABA Night League (Victoria and District Amateur Basketball Association) — nonprofit community league in Victoria; 188 teams, 1,800+ players, 92 games/week this season (self-published); software unknown
verification.evidence: Own site https://www.vicbball.com/ states verbatim: 'This season the V&DABA registered 188 teams with over 1800 players playing in 92 games per week' and 'V&DABA is a nonprofit organization based in Victoria, British Columbia, Canada' (scale figures remain self-published, as the claim already flags). INDEPENDENT: OrgBook BC (official registry, https://orgbook.gov.bc.ca) lists VICTORIA AND DISTRICT AMATEUR BASKETBALL ASSOCIATION as an ACTIVE registered society, S0009118 — nonprofit status corroborated. League structure (team-based registration, U-11/U-13/U-15/U-18, Oct–spring break) per https://www.vicbball.com/about-the-league.html. No registration/league software identified on the site — software stays unknown.

verification.claim: TCYBA Leagues (Tri-City Youth Basketball Association) — registered not-for-profit since 1999; 2,000+ players; software TeamSnap (registration links + teamsnap.com login); SportsEngine org directory listing; website on Wix
verification.evidence: INDEPENDENT: OrgBook BC lists TRI CITY YOUTH BASKETBALL ASSOCIATION as ACTIVE registered society S0041887; also appears on the PSO registry Basketball BC Member Clubs 2025-26 (https://basketball.bc.ca/clubs/member-clubs-2025-26/, contact Ross Tomlinson, Fraser River region). Own site https://www.tcyba.org/registration-info confirms 'volunteer-driven registered not-for-profit', 'formed in 1999', 'well over 2000 players, from Kindergarten to grade 12'. TeamSnap confirmed: 'Team Snap Registration' nav item, 'Registration Login' → teamsnap.com, plus a TeamSnap Sites presence at https://tricityyouthbasketballassociation.teamsnapsites.com/tcyba-home/. SportsEngine directory listing confirmed: https://www.sportsengine.com/org/tri-city-youth-basketball-association-145059. Wix confirmed (static.wixstatic.com assets throughout tcyba.org). Minor intel wrinkle: a tcyba.org registration page mentions confirmation emails from Stack Sports, suggesting the TeamSnap registration flow rides Stack Sports infrastructure or a legacy system — TeamSnap is the primary current platform.

verification.claim: RBL Basketball Leagues — private operator in Vancouver since 1997; scale unknown; software eSportsDesk (legacy rblbasketball.ca footer 'Design, Hosting, Online Registration & Administration tools by esportsdesk.com'); also on RecLeague.net

INDEPENDENT: OrgBook BC shows 'RBL BASKETBALL' as an ACTIVE registered firm FM0407378 (private operator, not a society; 'REAL BASKETBALL' FM0223016 also registered). eSportsDesk legacy site confirmed live: https://www.rblbasketball.ca/leagues/custom_page.cfm?clientid=1477&leagueid=0&pageid=14581 (page title carries the exact esportsdesk.com attribution). RecLeague.net presence confirmed: https://www.recleague.net/leagues/roundballvancouversp19/teams/50250. Note the current primary site is rblbasketball.com (WordPress-style), with the eSportsDesk .ca site as legacy.

verification.claim: Seymour Steve Nash Youth Basketball (Seymour Basketball Association), North Vancouver — operated by Seymour Basketball Association; software TechSys (registration portal at sba.techsys.tv/register)
verification.correction: Treat this entry as defunct/unverifiable and remove TechSys as current software. Direct test (HTTP and HTTPS) of sba.techsys.tv/register returns HTTP 404 — the registration portal is dead (the techsys.tv vendor itself is alive: a Spappz Sports Applications registration platform, so 'TechSys/Spappz = niche platform' was historically right). Worse, seymourbasketball.com — the org's supposed website — now belongs to an entirely different organization: Seymour Basketball Association of Seymour, Victoria, AUSTRALIA (Chittick Park, Pollard St, Seymour VIC 3660; email basketballseymour@gmail.com; registration via PlayHQ, the Australian platform). Any census row citing seymourbasketball.com or PlayHQ for a BC club is a name-collision error. No 'Seymour Basketball Association' entity exists in OrgBook BC and it is not on Basketball BC's 2025-26 member-club list.
verification.evidence: The only support for the North Vancouver org is the stale third-party directory https://www.vancouversports.com/basketball/seymour-steve-nash-youth-basketball.php (PO Box 42, #112-1151 Mt. Seymour Rd, North Vancouver BC V7H 2Y4; Info@SeymourBasketball.com; link http://sba.techsys.tv/register) — evidence the org and TechSys portal existed at some point. Contradicting current-state evidence: (1) curl http://sba.techsys.tv/register → HTTP 404 'Not Found' (and the HTTPS cert on that host is issued for ultrasoccerleague.com / ultrasoccerleague.spappz.com — server repurposed); (2) https://seymourbasketball.com/ fetched 2026-07-14 is the Australian club (Seymour VIC 3660, PlayHQ registration); (3) OrgBook BC search for 'Seymour Basketball' returns no basketball entity; (4) absent from https://basketball.bc.ca/clubs/member-clubs-2025-26/. Nearby lookalikes that must NOT be conflated: seymouryouthbasketball.com (Seymour, Tennessee — 'TN Tornadoes' team page) and seymourboysbasketballassociation.com (US).

verification.claim: Club 'Vancouver Eagles Youth Basketball' (Vancouver) exists; teams play in Vancouver Eagles Youth Basketball SNYB league and Vancouver Eagles U12 Winter League
verification.correction: Club existence and the U12 Winter League are CONFIRMED; the separate 'Vancouver Eagles SNYB league' entry should be dropped or marked historical. Record leagues as: U12 Winter League 2026 (Jan 5 - Mar 9, 2026) and U12 Spring League 2026, both hosted on RecLeague.net (software intel: RecLeague.net). The SNYB association appears only in a stale third-party directory, is absent from the club's current site, and the club's own RecLeague page calls the U12 Winter League 'our first exciting youth league for U12 players' — implying no pre-existing Eagles-run SNYB league.
verification.evidence: Club CONFIRMED: https://vancouvereagles.com/ (training + games for ages 6-15 across ~7 Vancouver school gyms incl. L'Ecole Bilingue, Maple Grove, Lord Nelson); OrgBook BC lists VANCOUVER EAGLES YOUTH BASKETBALL CLUB LTD. as ACTIVE corporation BC1542715. U12 Winter League CONFIRMED: https://www.recleague.net/leagues/thehomeleagueu12clubteam/teams/420342 ('Vancouver Eagles Youth Basketball Club | U12 Winter League 2026... Winter Season runs January 05 - March 09, 2026'); also U12 Spring League 2026 at https://www.recleague.net/sites/u12springleague/teams/434314. SNYB link only via directory https://www.vancouversports.com/basketball/vancouver-eagles (SNYB program, contact Michael Krakovsky, www.vancouvereagles.ca — that domain 301-redirects to vancouvereagles.com, so it is the same org); no SNYB mention anywhere on the current site.

verification.evidence: https://rblbasketball.com/ and https://rblbasketball.com/leagues/ — Vancouver youth basketball operator since 1997 running its own tiered 4v4/5v5 recreational and competitive leagues (also fields teams into the BC grade 7 provincial championships). INDEPENDENT: OrgBook BC registered firm 'RBL BASKETBALL' FM0407378 (ACTIVE); third-party league listing on RecLeague.net (https://www.recleague.net/leagues/roundballvancouversp19/teams/50250); profile at https://vancouver.kidsoutandabout.com/content/rbl-basketball. Type: private operator/academy running own leagues.

verification.claim: Club 'Vancouver Girls Basketball Association (VGBA)' (Vancouver) exists; teams play in VGBA Fall Pre-Season League and VGBA Spring League
verification.evidence: Own site http://vgba.ca/leagues/ details both leagues: Fall Pre-Season League (divisions Gr 7-9 and Gr 10-12, Britannia & Strathcona Community Centres, 7 Saturday nights Oct 8 - Nov 19, $600/team, contact Mitra) and Spring League (Gr 8-11, Apr-May, 8 weeks, $320/team, contact Mike Evans mevans@vsb.bc.ca); teams must be registered as Basketball BC club teams. INDEPENDENT: OrgBook BC lists VANCOUVER GIRLS BASKETBALL ASSOCIATION as ACTIVE registered society S0053162; active Facebook presence (https://www.facebook.com/vgba604/). Type: community association (nonprofit society) running own girls' leagues.

verification.claim: Club 'Night Hoops Basketball Society' (Vancouver) exists; teams play in Night Hoops' own leagues
verification.evidence: Own site https://www.nighthoops.ca/ / https://www.nighthoops.ca/about-us: established 1996, free late-night basketball leagues for youth 12-18 on Friday/Saturday nights, organized out of community centres, neighbourhood houses, community schools and youth custody services; also a Winter 3v3 League (Britannia and Sunset Community Centres). INDEPENDENT: CRA registered charity #868750142RR0001 (https://www.charitydata.ca/charity/night-hoops-basketball-society/868750142RR0001/); OrgBook BC ACTIVE society S0039391; City of Vancouver recreation system lists 'Night Hoops Basketball League (False Creek)' (https://anc.ca.apm.activecommunities.com/vancouver/activity/search/detail/555495). verification.evidence: Own site https://www.raincitybasketball.ca/ and https://www.raincitybasketball.ca/thedripleague: Drip League = in-house development league (ages U10-16, 3 sessions/week with weekend in-house league game, pathway to 'Purple' club rosters); club founded by Coaches Taylor and Emmy. INDEPENDENT: RecLeague.net listing (https://www.recleague.net/leagues/57075/teams/282990 'Raincity | Vancouver Competitive League'), parent-review profile https://www.chatterblock.com/resources/74352/raincity-basketball-club-vancouver-bc/, active Facebook/Instagram ('RainCity Basketball Club Inc.'). Type: private club/academy running an in-house league. (No BC-registry society found under 'Raincity' basketball — likely incorporated under Inc. name; existence not in doubt.)

pso.name: Basketball Alberta (Alberta Basketball Association / ABA)
pso.address: Percy Page Centre, 2nd floor, 11759 Groat Road, Edmonton, AB T5M 3K6
pso.software: RAMP InterActive end-to-end: main site 'Website by RAMP InterActive', RAMP games portals on subdomains (yp.abbasketball.ca Youth Provincials, clubinvitational.abbasketball.ca Club Championship, 3x3.abbasketball.ca, 4directions.abbasketball.ca), and RAMP registrations (e.g. eybaelite.rampregistrations.com for sanctioned programs). Also uses tourny.ca for 3x3 Championship bracket/scheduling.
pso.notes: CAUTION: the domain given in the brief, basketballalberta.ca, serves a misconfigured cert (*.cloudns.net) — canonical domain is abbasketball.ca. Staff: Dave Drabiuk (Executive Director), Jef Bailey (Programs & Events Director, ext 2), Natalie Hoy (Administration Coordinator, ext 3), Reagan Wood (Website & Information Specialist); staff emails not published on contact page. Member registry (who-are-members) lists: EYBA (with 10 constituent associations), RDMBA (Red Deer), LMBA (Lethbridge), plus ~26 direct member clubs (Aurora Basketball, Aviators, Calgary Grizzlies, Calgary Basketball Academy, CYDC, Calgary Thunder, Calgary Warriors, Coaldale BC, Cochrane Chaos, Edmonton Grads, Edson Raiders, Foundation Youth Basketball, Genesis Basketball, Junior Griffins, Junior Horns, Legends, Lethbridge Blazers, Red Jacket Premier Girls, Alberta Shooting Stars, Spruce Grove Knights, Visions Basketball Academy, West Elite, Trinity Athletic Assoc, SC Elite, Stettler BC, W.I.N. Basketball). CMBA has its own page on abbasketball.ca and appears in the ABA Games Portal league menu but is oddly absent from the current who-are-members text (likely a stale page). Historical registry snapshots exist at /content/2022-2023-clubs-amp-community-groups-in-good-standing and /content/2020-2021-club-organizations-in-good-standing. ABA also runs Team Alberta, Junior Academy, SNYB/Jr NBA programs and sanctions club tournaments (/content/club-tournaments).

leagues.name: CMBA Fall/Winter League (Calgary Minor Basketball Association)
leagues.operator: Calgary Minor Basketball Association (CMBA), nonprofit; 10 zone member associations
leagues.ages_tiers: Tykes (development), U11, U13, U15, U18 boys & girls; tiered divisions incl. a top 'Platinum League' tier; FIBA rules with age-group modifications
leagues.region: Calgary + surrounding (zones: Northwest, NCBC Thunder, EastPro, Calwest, SoCal, Bow River South, plus outlying Airdrie AMBA, Cochrane, CLS Chestermere/Langdon/Strathmore, Okotoks OBA)
leagues.scale: 10 zone member associations; 4,500 players play CMBA league basketball annually (self-published)
leagues.season: Fall/winter, roughly Oct–Mar; team registrations come from zone associations (players register through their zone)
leagues.software: RAMP InterActive website (cmba.ab.ca + cloud.rampinteractive.com file hosting) + TeamLinkt for league operations — league hubs at leagues.teamlinkt.com/calgaryminorbasketballassociation and a 'TeamLinkt CMBA Score Sheet' (digital gamesheets). Hybrid RAMP-site/TeamLinkt-league stack is notable competitive intel.
leagues.contact: league@cmba.ab.ca / cmba@cmba.ab.ca; (403) 804-3396; PO Box 24150 Calgary RPO, Evergreen, AB T2Y 0J9
leagues.notes: CMBA self-describes as 'Where youth basketball begins'. Runs Sizzling Summer Camps (U9-U15) and Holiday Hoopla winter mini-camp. Zone associations (e.g. Airdrie airdriebasketball.ca) are RAMP sites.

leagues.name: CMBA Spring League (club season)
leagues.operator: Calgary Minor Basketball Association (CMBA)
leagues.ages_tiers: U11, U13, U15, U18 boys & girls; club weeknight league + Weekend Tournament League; 'Club Premier League' tier appears in CMBA nav
leagues.scale: CMBA zone association teams + independent club teams (clubs reported entering include Rise, Genesis Basketball, Excel, Supreme Hoops, Ignite, Swoosh, Excel Basketball Academy)
leagues.season: Mid-April to mid-June (2025: weeknight league began Apr 14-16; Weekend Tournament League May 9-10 & May 30-31; playoffs Jun 2-4, finals Jun 9-10)
leagues.software: TeamLinkt Spring League Hub for scores/standings/full schedules (leagues.teamlinkt.com/calgaryminorbasketballassociation/SpringLeagueHub); 2026 Spring Tech Package also published on TeamLinkt; info pages on RAMP site
leagues.notes: This is THE Calgary club spring league — CMBA takes club-team registrations only in spring. Zones lend uniforms and buy AYBC insurance (via CMBA) so spring zone teams can rent gyms and enter outside tournaments (documented on Airdrie AMBA spring page).

leagues.name: CMBA Diamond Prep League (fall HS girls)
leagues.operator: Calgary Minor Basketball Association (CMBA)

leagues.season: Fall: 2025 dates Sept 20 – Nov 15 (Sundays: 12:00-1:15 practice/training + 1:25-3:00 games); ranking Sept 14
leagues.software: RAMP (info + registration on cmba.ab.ca)
leagues.notes: $400 + $20 non-refundable tryout fee. Fills the fall gap before school HS season for senior girls.

leagues.name: EYBA Fall/Winter League (Edmonton Youth Basketball Association)

leagues.name: EYBA Spring League
leagues.operator: Edmonton Youth Basketball Association (EYBA)
leagues.ages_tiers: 8 divisions: 11U (5v5 on 10-ft hoops), 13U, 15U, 18U boys & girls; two tracks — Community Affiliated (EYBA member teams only) and Open/Club (any club/independent teams); no overage players
leagues.scale: Team-entry league with per-division caps; guaranteed 10 games per team
leagues.season: 2026: play begins Mar 29, main round ends ~May 24, playoffs end ~Jun 14; registration opens early Jan, closes Mar 6; typically 1 game/week on fixed weeknights
leagues.software: RAMP (eyba.rampregistrations.com team registration; schedules on eyba.ca RAMP site)
leagues.notes: Open/Club fee $1,850/team incl. referees (EBOA-licensed officials). Venues: EYBA Centre, Saville Community Sports Centre, Legacy Athletics. This is the main Edmonton club spring league (Calgary equivalent = CMBA Spring).

leagues.name: Edmonton Youth Premier League (EYPL)

leagues.scale: Up to 60 players per age/gender category (240 max)

leagues.software: RAMP (eybaelite.rampregistrations.com); EBOA-officiated games, game video, weekly standings, player statistics

leagues.name: Alberta Basketball Youth Provincials (YP)
leagues.operator: Basketball Alberta (ABA)

leagues.region: Province-wide (2026 host: Calgary); entrants include CMBA zone teams (Bow, Calwest, SoCal, NCBC, EastPro, Okotoks, Cochrane, CLS), EYBA association teams (NW, NEBA, SEBA, PARK, SLAM, SBA, BYBA, LCBA) and rural/small-town teams (e.g. Magrath Rockets)
leagues.scale: Dozens of teams per age group across Div 1-4 (e.g. 11U boys alone fields 4 divisions of 6-8 teams on the portal)

leagues.software: RAMP games portal yp.abbasketball.ca (schedules, gamesheets, standings); a legacy bracketteam.com bracket also exists from a prior edition
leagues.notes: Entry fees 2026: $575 for 5v5 divisions, $275 for 18U girls 3x3. The championship tournament for community youth basketball in Alberta — effectively the season-capper for CMBA/EYBA winter leagues.

leagues.name: Alberta Basketball 4 Directions Invitational
leagues.operator: Basketball Alberta (ABA)

leagues.name: AYBC — Association of Alberta Youth Basketball Clubs (spring/summer club circuit)
leagues.operator: AYBC (volunteer club collective; membership free)

Brief called it 'Alberta Youth Basketball Conference ~50 clubs' — correct name is Association of Alberta Youth Basketball Clubs; the 50-club figure matches its published counter.

verification.claim: League "CMBA Fall/Winter League (Calgary Minor Basketball Association)" — operated by CMBA, nonprofit; 10 zone member associations; 4,500 players annually (self-published); software: RAMP website (cmba.ab.ca + cloud.rampinteractive.com files) + TeamLinkt for league operations (league hub at leagues.teamlinkt.com/calgaryminorbasketballassociation + 'TeamLinkt CMBA Score Sheet'); hybrid RAMP/TeamLinkt stack
verification.evidence: cmba.ab.ca/content/What-is-CMBA-Basketball states 10 Zone Member Associations and ~4,500 players annually; site footer 'Website by RAMP InterActive' with files on cloud.rampinteractive.com/calgaryminorbasketball (official score sheets, schedule PDFs). TeamLinkt side verified from CMBA's own homepage HTML: 'CMBA Zones' link to https://leagues.teamlinkt.com/calgaryminorbasketballassociation/390906 and a 'Game Sheet' anchor to the file 'TeamLinkt CMBA Score Sheet .pdf' (hosted on cloud.rampinteractive.com); TeamLinkt league pages indexed as 'Calgary Minor Basketball Association : Powered by TeamLinkt' (leagues.teamlinkt.com/calgaryminorbasketballassociation) and registration at app.teamlinkt.com/register/find/calgaryminorbasketballassociation?cid=62196. Independent corroboration: member zone NCBC Thunder's site thunderbasketball.ca ('one of 11 zones representing Calgary Minor Basketball'), zone-club sites linked from CMBA (cnwb.ca, sccba.ca, bowriverbasketball.com, eastprobasketball.ca, thunderbasketball.ca, okotoksbasketball.ca, airdriebasketball.ca, cochraneminorbasketball.com, clsminorbasketball.ca, southstoneybasketball.com), community-agency listing ab.211.ca/record/1125178 (supports nonprofit/community org), and legacy itSportsNet league pages (itsportsnet.com league.php leagueID=5233) showing prior platform. Hybrid RAMP-site/TeamLinkt-ops stack solidly real.
verification.correction: Zone count is transitioning to 11: CMBA announced South Stoney Basketball Association as its '11th community zone' competing from the 2026-27 Fall season (cmba.ab.ca/content/South-Stoney-Announcement; southstoneybasketball.com now linked from cmba.ab.ca), and NCBC Thunder's site already says 'one of 11 zones'. Also note the 'TeamLinkt CMBA Score Sheet' is a PDF gamesheet branded TeamLinkt but hosted on RAMP cloud; TeamLinkt digital gamesheets/live-scoring is marketed by TeamLinkt (teamlinkt.com/sports-management-software/game-sheets) — treat 'digital gamesheets in use' as inferred, not directly observed.

verification.claim: League "CMBA Spring League (club season)" — operated by CMBA; zone association teams + independent club teams (clubs incl. Rise, Genesis Basketball, Excel, Supreme Hoops, Ignite, Swoosh, Excel Basketball Academy); software: TeamLinkt Spring League Hub + 2026 Spring Tech Package on TeamLinkt; info pages on RAMP site
verification.evidence: TeamLinkt Spring League Hub indexed live: leagues.teamlinkt.com/calgaryminorbasketballassociation/SpringLeagueHub ('Calgary Minor Basketball Association - Spring League Hub'); '2026 Spring Tech Package' page on TeamLinkt at leagues.teamlinkt.com/calgaryminorbasketballassociation/423626 (index-verified with content: deadlines, coaching cert requirements, 12 medals/team). RAMP-side info pages live: cmba.ab.ca/content/2025-Spring-League-Technical-Package, cmba.ab.ca/content/league-spring-dates, cmba.ab.ca/content/league-spring-team-registration; CMBA states spring league 'is made up of CMBA Zone Association Teams and Independent Club Teams' with club-team-only registration. Club entries verified via CMBA team pages: Rise U15 Girls (cmba.ab.ca/team/7743/301/22783/173482), Genesis U13 Blue (cmba.ab.ca/team/9984/300/22781/230967), Ignite U15 (cmba.ab.ca/team/9984/302/22786/230991) and Ignite U16, Supreme Hoops U13/U15/U17 (e.g. cmba.ab.ca/team/6200/301/16924/131388), plus Excel Basketball Academy's own page 'CMBA Spring League' (excelbasketball.ca/content/cmba-spring-league).
verification.correction: Drop 'Swoosh' from the CMBA spring club list: no Swoosh team found in CMBA; CMBA has a team named 'Swish' (cmba.ab.ca/team/7743/302/4713/175437), and Swoosh Basketball Canada is an Edmonton club (swooshcanada.com, 17019 100th St Edmonton) that plays EYBA and runs its own Swoosh spring league — likely a name mix-up. Also 'Excel' and 'Excel Basketball Academy' are the same club (dedupe).

verification.evidence: The club exists and plays CMBA: thunderbasketball.ca (RAMP site, fetched) — 'NCBC Thunder is one of 11 zones representing Calgary Minor Basketball. We represent the zone of Northcentral Calgary, with over 600 youth players from ages 6-17'; NCBC team pages with CMBA league statistics (thunderbasketball.ca/team/9229/0/6814/199225/leaguestatistics); CMBA lists 'NCBC Thunder (North Central)' as a zone and links thunderbasketball.ca. pso.name: Basketball Manitoba Inc.
pso.address: Room 324, 145 Pacific Avenue, Winnipeg, MB R3B 2Z6 (Sport Manitoba building)
pso.software: Blogger CMS for entire web presence (subdomains like pt.basketballmanitoba.ca and clubdatabase.basketballmanitoba.ca 301-redirect to Blogger label pages); Google Forms for annual club-team registration AND provincial-team tryout registration; Google Sheets for championship results; payments by eTransfer/credit card. No RAMP/TeamLinkt/SportsEngine at PSO level — a modernization opening. League level is split: RAMP (WMBA, Peg City) vs TeamLinkt (WYBA).
pso.notes: PSO for basketball in Manitoba (also serves some Northwestern Ontario teams). Key staff: Adam Wedlake (Executive Director, adam@basketballmanitoba.ca), Tiara Licsi (club registrations/payments, tiara@basketballmanitoba.ca), Dan Becker (Manager High Performance). Club registry ('Basketball Club Directory', clubdatabase.basketballmanitoba.ca -> basketballmanitoba.ca/search/label/ClubDirectory) lists 19 active clubs with named contacts — mostly Winnipeg (Crossover, Attack, REFRESH, Rising Star, DElite, Atlas, SUMMIT, Unite, Attack South, Legacy Prep academy, Evolve, Ambush, Relentless, Downtown Dawgs) plus Metro Linkz (Brandon), Central Plains BC (Portage area), Selkirk Junior Royals (East). All club teams must register annually with the PSO via Google Form before league/tournament play (WMBA community and WYBA teams exempt — they register through their leagues). PSO also runs the Manitoba Provincial Team program (14U/15U/17U M+F; $40 tryout fee, ~$1,500 program fee 50% subsidized; Google Forms registration). Adult-only leagues excluded from this census but noted for platform intel: Winnipeg Basketball League (TeamLinkt), Peg City Senior League (RAMP), PBA Winnipeg adult/Filipino divisions. verification.claim: WMBA Fall/Winter Community Centre League is operated by Winnipeg Minor Basketball Association (WMBA), nonprofit est. 1997; 200+ teams across six leagues (grew from 40 teams / 12 community centres in 1997); software: RAMP InterActive — website, registration (rampregistrations.com/winnipegminorbasketball), official assigning, team communication app
verification.evidence: WMBA about page (https://www.wmba.ca/content/about-the-wmba) states est. 1997, 'tipped off with 40 teams, based out of 12 Community Centers,' now 'over 200 teams and offers 6 leagues.' Independently corroborated by 25th-anniversary history (https://www.wmba25.ca/about — founded 1997 by Mike Ruta, described as a not-for-profit youth basketball league; WMBA Foundation incorporated as separate non-profit 2004) and Basketball Manitoba (PSO) coverage (https://www.basketballmanitoba.ca/2024/07/winnipeg-minor-basketball-offering.html). RAMP confirmed: wmba.ca is a 'Website by RAMP InterActive' and the live registration link on https://wmba.ca/content/registration is https://www.rampregistrations.com/winnipegminorbasketball/.
verification.correction: Two caveats: (a) 'official assigning' and 'team communication app' appear only in the generic RAMP promotional footer present on every RAMP-hosted site — no WMBA-specific evidence of using those two RAMP products (referee queries go to a named human assignor, Reid Kenyon); treat those two software line-items as unverified. (b) One wmba25.ca account says the first season ran out of 7 community clubs vs the 12 community centres figure on wmba.ca — the club's own accounts differ slightly.

verification.claim: WMBA Spring Community League is operated by WMBA; scale not published separately (part of WMBA's 200+ team ecosystem); software: RAMP InterActive (same stack as fall/winter)
verification.evidence: Basketball Manitoba (PSO, independent of WMBA) announced 'WMBA Spring Community League Basketball Registration Opens Feb 15, 2026' (https://www.basketballmanitoba.ca/2026/02/wmba-spring-community-league-basketball.html); league pages live on WMBA's RAMP site (https://www.wmba.ca/content/wmba-community-league-spring-2026, https://wmba.ca/content/spring-community-league-basketball-registration). April–June season, teams by community-centre catchment, fees = $172 WMBA membership + ~$80-100 community club fee; no separate team count published — consistent with the claim. Registration runs through the same rampregistrations.com/winnipegminorbasketball portal.

verification.claim: WMBA x ELEVATE 3x3 Youth Summer League is operated by WMBA in partnership with Elevate Basketball; expanded across more sites for 2026; 'filling fast' per June 2025 PSO post; software: RAMP (WMBA registration system); individual registration with teammate requests
verification.evidence: PSO article 'WMBA and ELEVATE Launch Expanded 2026 3x3 Youth Summer League Across Winnipeg for Ages 8-17' (https://www.basketballmanitoba.ca/2026/05/wmba-and-elevate-launch-expanded-2026.html) confirms the WMBA-Elevate partnership, 2026 expansion (six outdoor community-court sites: Dakota, Whyte Ridge, Kirkfield Westwood, Corydon, Oxford Heights, Garden City), registration via RAMP Registrations, and quotes 'Participants register individually, with the option to request teammates during registration.' 2025 inaugural-season PSO posts also exist (https://www.basketballmanitoba.ca/2025/05/wmba-x-elevate-announce-summer-3x3.html, https://www.basketballmanitoba.ca/2025/07/wmba-x-elevate-3x3-youth-summer-league.html). Only the exact 'filling fast' phrasing from a June 2025 post was not re-located; nothing contradicts it.

verification.claim: Peg City Club Basketball League (CBL) is operated by Peg City Basketball (pegcityball.club), Basketball Manitoba-sanctioned; part of Peg City's 175+ competitive club teams (U10-U18) across CBL + Showcase; team fee $2,995 (2025-26); software: RAMP InterActive — website + registration (club admins register teams, parents register players)
verification.evidence: PSO article (https://www.basketballmanitoba.ca/2025/08/peg-city-basketball-opens-team.html) confirms 2025-26 CBL team registration at $2,995 for 10U-13U, min 14 games. Peg City's club-basketball overview (https://pegcityball.club/content/club-basketball-overview) states 'more than 175 competitive club teams' across U10-U18 with CBL (10U-13U) and Showcase (14U-17U) as the two leagues, under Basketball Manitoba oversight. CBL page (https://www.pegcityball.club/content/peg-city-cbl) confirms 'Club administrators register teams in the RAMP registration system' and 'Parents are sent a team invite and are required to register players in RAMP'; pegcityball.club is a 'Website by RAMP InterActive' (registration portal: rampregistrations.com/PegCityYouthLeague).

verification.claim: Peg City Showcase is operated by Peg City Basketball (pegcityball.club), Basketball Manitoba-sanctioned; part of Peg City's 175+ competitive club teams (U10-U18); software: RAMP InterActive — website + registration
verification.evidence: PSO articles confirm the Showcase: 2025 announcement for 14U-17U club teams (https://www.basketballmanitoba.ca/2025/08/peg-city-basketball-announces-2025.html) and Fall 2025 schedules with 118 club teams across four boys' and four girls' divisions at Sport Manitoba, Dakota Fieldhouse, and U of Winnipeg Duckworth Centre (https://www.basketballmanitoba.ca/2025/09/schedules-announced-for-peg-city.html). Site and registration on RAMP (pegcityball.club, 'Website by RAMP InterActive'); 175+ team figure from https://pegcityball.club/content/club-basketball-overview.

verification.claim: Sea Bears Youth League (SBYL) is operated by Peg City Basketball + Winnipeg Sea Bears (CEBL pro franchise); capacity-capped (registration closes early if capacity reached), numbers not published; software: RAMP InterActive (registration via pegcityball.club)
verification.evidence: PSO article 'Winnipeg Sea Bears and Peg City Basketball Announce Youth Spring League' (https://www.basketballmanitoba.ca/2025/01/winnipeg-sea-bears-and-peg-city.html) plus the Sea Bears' own page (https://www.seabears.ca/sea-bears-youth-league) confirm the partnership; the league is 'fully managed and operated by Peg City Basketball' for ages 7-14 (Elite + Developmental divisions). Capacity cap confirmed: registration 'will close on February 12th or as soon as capacity is reached.' Hosted/registered on Peg City's RAMP stack (https://www.pegcityball.club/content/Sea-Bears-Youth-League; https://rampregistrations.com/PegCityYouthLeague/). No participation numbers published.

verification.claim: Manitoba Basketball Club Championships (provincials) operated by Basketball Manitoba (PSO); 131 club teams in 2026 over three weekends; $575/team entry (2025) with rural travel reductions; web registration with payment by eTransfer or credit card; results via Google Sheets; site on Blogger
verification.evidence: All elements independently verified on the PSO's own operational pages: 2025 fee '$575 per team (with rural travel reductions offered)' (https://www.basketballmanitoba.ca/2025/02/2025-manitoba-basketball-club.html); 2026 announcement confirms payment 'via eTransfer or credit card' and rural reductions (https://www.basketballmanitoba.ca/2026/01/2026-manitoba-basketball-club.html); 'A total of 131 club teams are participating... over three weekends' with results published at a public Google Sheets link (docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/e/2PACX-1vRzPZbQVWqHtNFcwZNrr3mcA9mgQRS3COB1hxufdByNT_KqOuNhqtNCpbMKqZ2sMCqQoRuBOk6EBJvk/pubhtml) (https://www.basketballmanitoba.ca/2026/05/2026-manitoba-basketball-club.html); basketballmanitoba.ca is Blogger-hosted (blogger.googleusercontent.com assets, Blogger template, /YYYY/MM/ post URLs). Note: fee rose to $600/team for 2026 — the claim's $575 is correctly labeled as the 2025 fee.

verification.claim: Westman Youth Basketball Association (WYBA) leagues & programs, contact Juan Mosquera; Club Tournament: 6 divisions, 300+ athletes (2026); league team counts not published; software: TeamLinkt — website (wyba.ca) + registration (wyba.ca/registrations)
verification.evidence: The '6 divisions and over 300 athletes' text comes from WYBA's TeamLinkt news item about the 2024 tournament (https://wyba.ca/leagues/NewsItem/13462/22837 — 'WYBA 2024 Tournament schedule... 6 divisions and over 300 athletes,' games Fri May 17 2024 at Brandon University HLC and Crocus Plains). The 2026 Club Tournament as announced by the PSO (https://www.basketballmanitoba.ca/2026/01/westman-youth-basketball-2026-club.html) is BOYS ONLY with 3 divisions (U13/U15/U18), initial cap of 12 teams per division (max ~36 teams), $700 early-bird/$750 regular, May 15-17, 2026, contact wybamanitoba@hotmail.com. The 2025 edition had 4 divisions (B U15/U17, G U16/U18, $600, cap 8/division). Everything else in the claim held up: WYBA exists (Brandon, MB nonprofit), Juan Mosquera is WYBA Director & Head Coach of Programming (independent source: https://gobobcats.ca/sports/womens-basketball/roster/coaches/juan-mosquera/150), league team counts not published, and TeamLinkt is confirmed (wyba.ca is 'Powered by TeamLinkt'; wyba.ca/registrations resolves, with registration flow at app.teamlinkt.com/register/find/westmanyouthbasketballassociation).
verification.correction: The 2026 WYBA Club Tournament was announced as boys-only with 3 divisions (U13/U15/U18), 12-team cap per division (max ~36 teams), $700-750/team, May 15-17 2026 in Brandon. The '6 divisions, 300+ athletes' figure describes the May 2024 tournament (which included girls' divisions), not 2026. Attribute scale as: '2024 tournament: 6 divisions, 300+ athletes; 2026 tournament: boys-only, 3 divisions, capped at 12 teams/division.'
