pso.name: Basketball Saskatchewan Inc. (BSI)
pso.software: Website on Squarespace; program/event registration on RAMP InterActive (BSIPrograms.rampregistrations.com); its SKY Basketball rural Jr. NBA program runs on TeamLinkt (skybasketball.ca)
pso.notes: PSO for basketball in Saskatchewan. Member registry publicly lists: 7 youth organizations/leagues (RCBA, SMBA, Living Skies Indigenous Basketball League, HoopLife Regina, Regina Youth Club Basketball League, Moose Jaw Minor Basketball League, Prince Albert Community Basketball League), 60+ competitive rep clubs (e.g. Ballers Basketball, Regina Raptors, 306 Heat, Xcite Athletics, Saskatoon Slam orgs, Greenwave United, Yorkton Hotshots, Estevan Club Basketball, Warman Wolves, Melville Mambas), 7 adult house leagues (out of scope), plus training programs (Masterpiece Basketball, Xcite Athletics). Runs BSI Club Championships (provincials) and SKY Basketball in 40+ rural communities; oversees basketball at biennial Saskatchewan Games; hosting Canada Basketball U15/U17 Boys Nationals in Regina, Aug 2-8 2026. Office hours Mon-Fri 9:00-4:30. Competitive-intel headline: TeamLinkt utterly dominates SK youth basketball (SMBA, RCBA, RYCBL, LSIBL, SKY, plus tournament registrations); RAMP holds the PSO's own registrations and PACBA; LeagueApps (Golden Ticket Moose Jaw) and Citrus Camps (HoopLife, Swift Current Surge club) have niche footholds.

leagues.name: Saskatoon Minor Basketball Association (SMBA)
leagues.operator: SMBA — non-profit community association, est. 1952 (originally Saskatoon Church Basketball League, renamed 1985); volunteer board (President Greg Jockims)
leagues.ages_tiers: Ages 5-18 / Grades 1-12: Dunkaroos (Gr 1-2 intro), Community Association Leagues (Gr 3-12, teams formed by Saskatoon community associations, Saturday games), NLDL Next Level Development League (citywide premier tier, Girls/Boys Mini Gr 5-6 and Bantam Gr 7-8, 10-12 players/team, 1 game + 2 practices/week), Jr/Sr Girls and Jr/Sr Boys leagues; spring/summer: Elevate & Excel, HoopStart, 3x3 League, camps
leagues.region: Saskatoon + surrounding area (Martensville and Warman players may try out for NLDL)
leagues.scale: City-wide, community-association-driven; dozens of community associations field teams (e.g. Brevoort Park, Silverwood Heights); no published player total
leagues.season: Fall/Winter core league ~Oct-Mar (registration opens early July); Spring/Summer 3x3 league and programs Apr-Jun
leagues.software: TeamLinkt (leagues.teamlinkt.com/smba, smbayxe.ca, app.teamlinkt.com/register/find/smba); legacy sites show prior migrations from LeagueToolbox and GOALLINE (smba.ca)
leagues.notes: Largest youth basketball operation in Saskatchewan's largest city; the biggest single TeamLinkt displacement target in the province. Community-association feeder structure is unusual (teams built by neighbourhood associations, SMBA schedules centrally).

leagues.name: Regina Community Basketball Association (RCBA)
leagues.operator: RCBA — non-profit community organization, founded 1931; bills itself the longest-running league in western Canada
leagues.ages_tiers: Ages 5-17/18: Jr. Cougars (Kindergarten), Gr 1-2, Gr 3, Gr 4, Gr 5-6, Gr 7-8, High School divisions, plus RCBA All-Stars program; all skill levels, Basketball Canada LTAD-aligned
leagues.scale: City-wide house league across all grade bands; no published player total
leagues.season: Fall/Winter (approx. Oct-Mar)
leagues.software: TeamLinkt (rcba.ca is a TeamLinkt-powered site; registration at app.teamlinkt.com/register/find/rcba)
leagues.notes: Regina's main recreational/house youth league; complement to the club-only RYCBL.

leagues.name: Regina Youth Club Basketball League (RYCBL)
leagues.operator: RYCBL (independent league for club teams; registered member of Basketball Saskatchewan)
leagues.ages_tiers: Club teams only: Grade 4/5, 5/6, 6/7, 7/8, Grade 8, and High School divisions; rosters 6-14 players; clubs supply coaches and set rosters
leagues.scale: Multiple divisions of club (rep) teams; no published team count
leagues.season: 5-on-5 winter league Nov-Mar (games at Tuxedo Courts); 3-on-3 spring league (games at The Cage / Sheldon-Williams Courts)
leagues.software: TeamLinkt (leagues.teamlinkt.com/rycbl) + WordPress front site (reginayouthclubbasketball.com — SSL cert currently expired)
leagues.notes: This is the game-circuit for Regina rep clubs (Ballers, Regina Raptors, 306 Heat etc.) between tournaments — the closest SK analogue to a GTA club league. Website is a 'temporary page until our website is complete' — potentially receptive to a better platform.

leagues.name: Living Skies Indigenous Basketball League (LSIBL)
leagues.operator: Aboriginal Friendship Centres of Saskatchewan (AFCS) in partnership with One Love Basketball Inc., with Government of Canada funding
leagues.ages_tiers: Indigenous youth ages 11-17: Junior (11-14) and Senior (15-17) divisions, boys and girls; free to register
leagues.region: Province-wide in 3 divisions — North (Buffalo Narrows, Ile-a-la-Crosse, La Loche, Meadow Lake), Central (Battlefords, La Ronge, Prince Albert, Saskatoon), South (Regina, Qu'Appelle Valley, Yorkton)
leagues.scale: Up to 650 athletes per season; 1,700+ registrants cumulative by 2021 reporting; AFCS/Jumpstart materials cite 3,500+ athletes served; 44-88 coaches and 22+ officials certified
leagues.season: September-March, ending with a Provincial Championship; founded 2020, league play began fall 2021/spring 2022
leagues.software: TeamLinkt (separate Junior and Senior registration links); site on Wix (livingskiesbasketball.com)
leagues.contact: Via livingskiesbasketball.com; League Manager Paige Crozon, South Coordinator Avery Pearce
leagues.notes: Saskatchewan's first province-wide Indigenous youth league; grant-funded, cost-free model; BSI member. Distinct multi-community logistics (hub communities, cultural workshops alongside games).

leagues.name: HoopLife League (Regina, fall/winter)
leagues.operator: HoopLife Basketball — private academy/business ('The #1 Basketball Program in Regina'), own facility HoopLife Centre, 661 Solomon Crescent, Regina; also operates in Saskatoon
leagues.ages_tiers: Boys and girls Grades 1-8 (core league marketed as Gr 3-8, entry via tryout clinics); paid, trained coaches only — no volunteers; $750/season all-in
leagues.scale: Multiple grade/gender divisions at a single dedicated facility; no published team count
leagues.season: October-April
leagues.software: Citrus Camps (register.citruscamps.com) for all registrations; marketing site custom/Webflow-style
leagues.notes: Private-academy league model (premium price point ~3x community leagues); BSI member. One of only two Citrus Camps users found in SK.

leagues.name: HoopLife Spring League (Regina)
leagues.operator: HoopLife Basketball (same private academy as above)
leagues.ages_tiers: Boys and girls Grades 1-8 across 8 divisions; includes optional 10-session skills add-on
leagues.scale: 8 divisions; no published team count
leagues.season: Mid-April to mid-June
leagues.software: Citrus Camps (register.citruscamps.com)
leagues.notes: Also runs an adult 3x3 rec league (16+) year-round — out of scope but shows facility utilization model.

leagues.name: Moose Jaw Minor Basketball League (Golden Ticket Sports)
leagues.operator: Golden Ticket Sports (private operator; co-owner Tanner Brightman) at Golden Ticket Sports Centre, 1599 Main St N, Moose Jaw — took over after the volunteer Moose Jaw Minor Basketball Association disbanded in 2023
leagues.ages_tiers: Kindergarten-Grade 9: Rugrat (Gr 2-3), Bantam (Gr 4-6), Junior (Gr 6-9); plus Canada Basketball/Jr. NBA program for K-Gr 2; ~$275+GST (2025-26), one weekday practice + Saturday/Sunday games
leagues.scale: City-wide minor league (Moose Jaw pop. ~34K); no published player count
leagues.season: Late October to mid-February; optional pre-season clinics
leagues.software: LeagueApps (sportscentre.leagueapps.com — 'Moose Jaw Minor Basketball - Fall 2025'); TeamLinkt links used for athlete/coach registration in 2023 takeover season; tournaments via bracketteam.com
leagues.notes: Case study: volunteer association collapsed (2023) and a private facility operator absorbed the league. Old domain mjmba.com now appears dead/hijacked. Only LeagueApps footprint found in SK youth basketball.

leagues.name: Prince Albert Community Basketball Association (PACBA) Winter League
leagues.operator: PACBA — non-profit community organization, established 2009 (listed in BSI registry as Prince Albert Community Basketball League)
leagues.ages_tiers: Ages 5+ / K-high school: Winter League boys & girls 11U, 13U, 15U; Jr. NBA program Gr 1-3 (8 weeks, Mar-May); Lakers spring rep season 11U/13U/15U (Gr 3-6 team registration)
leagues.scale: City league; no published counts ('second season' of relaunched youth league per paNOW, Sept 2024)
leagues.season: Winter League October-February; Jr. NBA and Lakers spring programs March-May/June
leagues.software: RAMP InterActive (pacommunitybasketball.com 'Website by RAMP InterActive' — registration on RAMP)
leagues.contact: Via pacommunitybasketball.com / Facebook (no public email/phone found)
leagues.notes: Only RAMP-based youth basketball league found in SK (RAMP otherwise appears in SK for other sports and the PSO's own registrations).

leagues.name: SKY Basketball / Saskatchewan Youth Basketball (Jr. NBA)
leagues.operator: Basketball Saskatchewan Inc. (PSO-run rural development league/program), delivered through local community coordinators
leagues.ages_tiers: Kindergarten-Grade 6 in grade bands (K-2, Gr 3-4, Gr 5-6, gender-split in older grades); ~$65-75 incl. basketball; KidSport-subsidized in some communities
leagues.region: 40+ rural communities grouped in areas: Melfort (9 communities), Moosomin (8), North Battleford (12), Outlook (6), Swift Current (7), Weyburn (9), Yorkton (1)
leagues.scale: 40+ communities, each with its own coordinator; no published player total
leagues.season: Winter sessions (roughly Jan-Mar) with area year-end tournaments April-May
leagues.software: TeamLinkt (skybasketball.ca is a TeamLinkt-powered site; per-community registration)
leagues.notes: The PSO's own grassroots league for small-town SK. Community list/session dates on the TeamLinkt Communities page include stale 2020 references — per-area community counts should be re-verified at registration time.

leagues.name: BSI Club Championships (Basketball Saskatchewan Provincials)
leagues.operator: Basketball Saskatchewan Inc.
leagues.ages_tiers: Club teams: 11U, 13U, 15U, and Open High School divisions; rosters of 12 players + 2 coaches; $400/team (11U), $550/team (others); minimum 3 games guaranteed
leagues.region: Province-wide (2026 edition hosted in Regina, May 22-24)
leagues.scale: Season-ending championship for the province's 60+ registered rep clubs; team counts not published
leagues.season: Annual, late May (2026: May 22-24; registration Nov 1 2025-May 4 2026)
leagues.software: RAMP registrations (BSIPrograms.rampregistrations.com)
leagues.notes: The anchor event of the SK rep-club calendar; the reason clubs affiliate with BSI.

leagues.name: BSI-sanctioned spring club tournament circuit (2026)
leagues.operator: Multiple: Golden Ticket Sports (May Madness, Regina, May 1-3), Misfits (Misfits Invitational, Regina, May 8-10), GT Thunder (Prince Albert, May 8-10), Beardy's & Okemasis Cree Nation (Indigenous Youth Basketball Championships, Warman, May 8-10, ffavel@beardysband.com), Moose Jaw Spartans (Premier Prairie Classic, May 15-17, mjspartans17@gmail.com), Battle in the Bridge City (Saskatoon, May 29-31), Basketball Experience (3x3, Saskatoon, June 12, info@basketballexperience.ca), Get Your Game On (Regina, May 1-3); clubs also cite the Rattlers/Mamba All Sask Classic
leagues.ages_tiers: Rep club teams, typically U11-U18 / grade-based divisions (varies by event)
leagues.region: Regina, Saskatoon, Moose Jaw, Prince Albert, Warman
leagues.scale: 8+ BSI-sanctioned tournaments in May-June 2026; this is the competitive season for SK's 60+ rep clubs (e.g. Swift Current Surge lists Premier Prairie Classic, All Sask Classic, May Madness as its season)
leagues.season: May-June annually, feeding into BSI Club Championships
leagues.software: Split: bracketteam.com (Golden Ticket events, GT Thunder, Battle in the Bridge City) and TeamLinkt (Get Your Game On, Misfits Invitational)
leagues.contact: Per-event (see operator field); sanction list maintained by BSI
leagues.notes: SK has no year-round provincial club league — rep clubs practice all winter (many players also in SMBA/RCBA/RYCBL) then compete via this May-June tournament circuit + provincials. Aggregate entry; individual tournaments are weekend events, not standing leagues.

leagues.name: Saskatchewan Summer Games — Basketball
leagues.operator: Saskatchewan Games Council, with Basketball Saskatchewan as sport governing body (contact Megan Penno)
leagues.ages_tiers: U14 (14 and under as of Dec 31; e.g. 2010-2011 births for 2024); male and female district teams of 10 athletes + coach + manager
leagues.region: Province-wide, athletes compete for geographic district teams (2024 Games hosted in Lloydminster)
leagues.scale: One team per gender per district; multi-sport Games format
leagues.season: Biennial summer Games (basketball played over ~4 days in late July)
leagues.software: Sask Games Council site (saskgames.ca); team selection run by districts/BSI
leagues.notes: Not a club league — district all-star competition — included for landscape completeness since it drives spring ID camps and district team programs.

verification.claim: SMBA (Saskatoon) — non-profit, est. 1952 as Saskatoon Church Basketball League, renamed 1985; volunteer board with President Greg Jockims; dozens of community associations field teams (Brevoort Park, Silverwood Heights); software TeamLinkt, legacy LeagueToolbox and GOALLINE at smba.ca
verification.evidence: About page (https://leagues.teamlinkt.com/smba/AbouttheSMBA) states est. 1952 as Saskatoon Church Basketball League, SMBA name embraced 1985, non-profit, Board of Directors with President Greg Jockims. Jockims' SMBA presidency independently corroborated by USask Huskie Athletics partnership announcement (https://huskies.usask.ca/news/2024/10/16/general-huskie-athletics-announces-partnership-with-saskatoon-minor-basketball-association.aspx) and CEBL/Saskatoon Mamba coaching announcements (https://www.cebl.ca/saskatoon-mamba-announce-key-coaching-additions-for-2026-season). BSI member registry lists SMBA (https://www.basketballsask.com/member-organizations/currentmembers). Community-association model verified: ~50 associations on https://leagues.teamlinkt.com/smba/LinkstoCommunityAssociations including both Brevoort Park and Silverwood Heights. TeamLinkt confirmed (leagues.teamlinkt.com/smba, smbayxe.ca/smba/ElevateandExcelSpringProgram, app.teamlinkt.com/register/find/smba). Legacy platforms both live-indexed: LeagueToolbox at http://www.smba.ca/site and smba.leaguetoolbox.com; GOALLINE at http://smba.ca/exec.php and smba.goalline.ca.

verification.claim: RCBA (Regina) — non-profit founded 1931, longest-running league in western Canada; city-wide house league; software TeamLinkt (rcba.ca TeamLinkt-powered, registration app.teamlinkt.com/register/find/rcba)
verification.evidence: Independent SkillShark case study (https://skillshark.com/case-studies/regina-community-basketball-association/) states established 1931 and 'the oldest youth sports league in Western Canada', ~1,200 kids onboarded (grades 5-8 batch). Own site rcba.ca pages are 'Powered by TeamLinkt' (https://www.rcba.ca/rcba/AboutUs) with registration page at https://leagues.teamlinkt.com/rcba/RegisterHere; formerly Regina Church League Basketball, renamed 2000. BSI member registry lists 'Regina Community Basketball Association (RCBA)' (https://www.basketballsask.com/member-organizations/currentmembers). Bonus intel: legacy itSportsNet page exists (http://www.itsportsnet.com/league.php?scriptName=HOME&leagueID=15644).

verification.claim: RYCBL (Regina) — independent league for club teams, registered member of Basketball Saskatchewan; multiple grade divisions; software TeamLinkt (leagues.teamlinkt.com/rycbl) + WordPress front site with currently expired SSL cert
verification.evidence: BSI member registry independently lists 'Regina Youth Club Basketball League' (https://www.basketballsask.com/member-organizations/currentmembers). TeamLinkt league site live (https://leagues.teamlinkt.com/rycbl/Schedule). Own site (https://reginayouthclubbasketball.com/) describes club-team league, grades 3-8 plus high school division, Nov-Mar. Expired SSL verified directly by TLS handshake on 2026-07-14: curl returns 'SSL certificate problem: certificate has expired' for reginayouthclubbasketball.com.

verification.claim: LSIBL — operated by AFCS in partnership with One Love Basketball Inc. with Government of Canada funding; up to 650 athletes/season, 1,700+ cumulative registrants by 2021 reporting, 3,500+ athletes served, 44-88 coaches and 22+ officials certified; TeamLinkt with separate Junior/Senior registration; Wix site
verification.evidence: Operator/funding corroborated by AFCS (https://afcs.ca/initiative/living-skies-basketball-league/) and Canada Basketball (https://www.basketball.ca/news/living-skies-indigenous-basketball-leagues-becomes-the-latest-canada-basketball-unified-partner — also cites 3,500+ athletes and 700+ participants/60 volunteer coaches a year). About page (https://www.livingskiesbasketball.com/about-lsibl) confirms separate TeamLinkt Junior (cid=48619) and Senior (cid=61614) registration links and '44-88+ coaches and 22+ officials' certification goal; Wix confirmed via wixstatic.com asset URLs. 1,700+ cumulative registrants and ~300 senior-division provincial athletes per Canada Basketball (https://www.basketball.ca/news/living-skies-indigenous-basketball-league-leading-the-way-in-saskatchewan, June 21, 2023). Also Eagle Feather News Jan 2026 (https://eaglefeathernews.com/2026/01/21/indigenous-basketball-league-supports-thousands-of-youth/).
verification.correction: Minor attribution fixes: the 1,700+ cumulative figure comes from June 2023 Canada Basketball reporting (which dates the league's founding to 2020), not '2021 reporting'; recent reporting cites 700+ participants per year rather than 'up to 650'.

verification.claim: HoopLife League (Regina fall/winter) — operated by HoopLife Basketball, private academy ('#1 Basketball Program in Regina'), own facility HoopLife Centre at 661 Solomon Crescent; also operates in Saskatoon; multiple grade/gender divisions; registrations on Citrus Camps
verification.evidence: Own site tagline 'The #1 Basketball Program In Regina' (https://www.hooplifebasketball.com/regina/home); HoopLife Centre facility at 661 Solomon Crescent with league divisions by grade/gender, boys and girls grades 3-8, BSI-sanctioned (https://www.hooplifebasketball.com/regina/leagues/hooplife-league, https://www.hooplifebasketball.com/regina/facility). Saskatoon operation confirmed at Sask Polytech (https://www.hooplifebasketball.com/saskatoon/home). All registrations via Citrus Camps (e.g. https://register.citruscamps.com/pg/pg_G7qJ0U7dKGC0Pqqh/evt/pg_evt_peDelsfOUFmZpKui). BSI member registry lists 'HoopLife Basketball (Regina)' under Youth (https://www.basketballsask.com/member-organizations/currentmembers).

verification.claim: HoopLife Spring League (Regina) — operated by HoopLife Basketball; 8 divisions; software Citrus Camps
verification.evidence: Official page (https://www.hooplifebasketball.com/regina/leagues/spring-league) states verbatim: 'A Competitive Youth Basketball Spring League in Regina with 8 divisions for Boys and Girls!' — boys grades 1-8 play 5v5, girls grades 1-8 play 3x3, mid-April to mid-June. Registration via Citrus Camps (https://register.citruscamps.com/pg/pg_G7qJ0U7dKGC0Pqqh?type=camp&tags=Spring+League&place_name=Regina). Note: an adversarial check surfaced a '7 competitive divisions' snippet elsewhere, but the current official spring-league page says 8.

verification.claim: Moose Jaw Minor Basketball League — run by Golden Ticket Sports (private, co-owner Tanner Brightman) at Golden Ticket Sports Centre, 1599 Main St N, after volunteer MJMBA disbanded 2023; software LeagueApps (sportscentre.leagueapps.com Fall 2025), TeamLinkt used in 2023 takeover season; tournaments via bracketteam.com
verification.evidence: Independent local news: MJMBA disbanding + Golden Ticket takeover, DiscoverMooseJaw Feb 15, 2023 (https://www.discovermoosejaw.com/articles/moose-jaw-minor-basketball-association-disbands-golden-ticket-sports-planning-fall-league) and MooseJawToday (https://www.moosejawtoday.com/local-news/minor-b-ball-association-closes-new-league-to-start-next-fall-6554021). Sept 6, 2023 DiscoverMooseJaw article (https://discovermoosejaw.com/articles/golden-ticket-sports-running-minor-basket-league-this-fall--winter) names co-owner Tanner Brightman, venue 1599 Main St N, and gives 2023 TeamLinkt registration links (app.teamlinkt.com/register/go/goldenticketsports/37606 athlete, /37616 coach). LeagueApps confirmed: 'Moose Jaw Minor Basketball - Fall 2025' at https://sportscentre.leagueapps.com/camps/4684965-moose-jaw-minor-basketball---fall-2025. bracketteam.com usage not independently re-verified but not contradicted; Golden Ticket Basketball Club also appears in the BSI member registry.

verification.claim: PACBA Winter League (Prince Albert) — non-profit est. 2009, listed in BSI registry as Prince Albert Community Basketball League; city league in 'second season' of relaunch per paNOW Sept 2024; software RAMP InterActive
verification.evidence: paNOW (independent), Sept 1, 2024: 'Youth basketball league returning for second season' (https://panow.com/2024/09/01/youth-basketball-league-returning-for-second-season/). Own site is 'Website by RAMP InterActive' (https://pacommunitybasketball.com/) — non-profit est. 2009, winter league Oct-Feb with 11U/13U/15U boys and girls divisions; AGM minutes hosted on cloud.rampinteractive.com (https://cloud.rampinteractive.com/PrinceAlbertCommunityBasketball/files/PACBA%20Annual%20General%20Meeting%20(AGM)%20Minutes%20-%20Nov%202,%202024%20(4).pdf). BSI member registry lists 'Prince Albert Community Basketball League' exactly as claimed (https://www.basketballsask.com/member-organizations/currentmembers).

verification.claim: Club 'SMBA' (Saskatoon) exists; its teams play in SMBA's own community and rep leagues and in BSI Club Championships (Basketball Saskatchewan Provincials)
verification.evidence: Entity and own-league operation fully confirmed: SMBA runs Community Association Leagues, the Next Level Development League (NLDL), and Jr/Sr Boys and Girls competitive divisions (https://leagues.teamlinkt.com/smba/AbouttheSMBA), and is a BSI member listed under Youth (https://www.basketballsask.com/member-organizations/currentmembers). However, no independent evidence was found of SMBA team entries in BSI's Provincial Open Club Championships (open to BSI member clubs, 13U/15U/open HS per https://www.basketballsask.com/players/tournaments); SMBA is listed by BSI as a youth league, not in the Clubs section. Nothing contradicts, but the provincials-participation component is uncorroborated — treat SMBA as a league operator rather than a rep club in the pipeline.

verification.claim: Club 'Saskatoon SLAM Basketball' exists; teams play in BSI Club Championships / BSI-sanctioned spring club tournament circuit
verification.evidence: Registered non-profit club, boys and girls U11/U13/U15/U18 (https://saskatoonslam.com/ and https://saskatoonslam.com/about/). BSI member registry independently lists four Slam entries under Clubs: 'Saskatoon Slam Storm', 'Saskatoon Slam Magic', 'Saskatoon Slam Suns', 'Saskatoon Slam Phantoms' (https://www.basketballsask.com/member-organizations/currentmembers). Spring club tournament circuit corroborated by Basketball Manitoba's posting of the Saskatoon Slam Prairie Season Opener Club Basketball Tournament (https://www.basketballmanitoba.ca/2022/03/saskatoon-slam-prairie-season-opener.html); BSI's Provincial Open Club Championships are open to BSI member clubs.

verification.evidence: Club exists: Facebook page (https://www.facebook.com/p/Shadow-Basketball-Club-100076524244017/), TeamLinkt registration (https://app.teamlinkt.com/register/go/saskatoonshadowbasketballclub/73399), Instagram, U11-U18 nonprofit boys club. Genesis Basketball tournament entry verified verbatim as 'Saskatoon Shadow (Wiebe)' on Genesis's LeagueApps site (https://genesisbasketball.leagueapps.com/tournaments/1025392/teams/2242546). Caveat: Shadow was not found on the BSI current-members page fetched 2026-07-14 (https://www.basketballsask.com/member-organizations/currentmembers), so the 'BSI-sanctioned' descriptor rests on tournament participation rather than listed membership.

verification.claim: Club 'YXE Elite Basketball Club' (Saskatoon) exists; plays BSI-sanctioned club tournament circuit
verification.evidence: Club site https://www.yxeelitebasketball.com/ (founded 2022, Saskatoon); BSI member registry independently lists 'YXE Elite' under Clubs (https://www.basketballsask.com/member-organizations/currentmembers); runs the YXE Elite Summer Jam tournament with registration on bracketteam.com (https://bracketteam.com/event/5602/YXE_Elite_Summer_Jam/registration) — note bracketteam is another platform datapoint for competitive intel.

verification.claim: SMBA is a non-profit community association est. 1952 (originally Saskatoon Church Basketball League, renamed 1985); volunteer board with President Greg Jockims; city-wide, community-association-driven (e.g. Brevoort Park, Silverwood Heights); no published player total; software TeamLinkt (leagues.teamlinkt.com/smba, smbayxe.ca, app.teamlinkt.com/register/find/smba); legacy LeagueToolbox and GOALLINE sites at smba.ca
verification.evidence: Est. 1952 / Church Basketball League origin / 1985 rename per SMBA about page (https://leagues.teamlinkt.com/smba/AbouttheSMBA) and SMBA Facebook ('Since 1952'). Greg Jockims named as SMBA President in an INDEPENDENT source: USask Huskie Athletics partnership announcement, Oct 16 2024 (https://huskies.usask.ca/news/2024/10/16/general-huskie-athletics-announces-partnership-with-saskatoon-minor-basketball-association.aspx), which also confirms non-profit status. Community-association-driven independently corroborated by the associations' own sites: https://brevoortpark.ca/basketball/ (Brevoort Park is SMBA Zone 6) and https://www.silverwoodcommunity.org/programs/basketball (runs basketball through SMBA); SMBA hosts a 'Links to Community Associations' page (https://leagues.teamlinkt.com/smba/LinkstoCommunityAssociations). TeamLinkt verified live: leagues.teamlinkt.com/smba, smbayxe.ca/smba/Standings (TeamLinkt markers in HTML), app.teamlinkt.com/register/find/smba returns HTTP 200. Legacy platforms both still live: http://smba.ca/page.php?page_id=117315 ('Powered by GOALLINE') and http://www.smba.ca/site ('Powered by LeagueToolbox'). BSI member registry lists 'Saskatoon Minor Basketball Association (SMBA)' under House Leagues - Youth (https://www.basketballsask.com/member-organizations/currentmembers). No player total published anywhere found.

verification.claim: RCBA is a non-profit community organization founded 1931, bills itself the longest-running league in western Canada; city-wide house league across all grade bands; no published player total; software TeamLinkt (rcba.ca is TeamLinkt-powered; registration at app.teamlinkt.com/register/find/rcba)
verification.evidence: RCBA About page (https://www.rcba.ca/rcba/AboutUs — page title 'Regina Community Basketball Association : Powered by TeamLinkt') states established 1931, previously Regina Church League Basketball (renamed 2000), non-profit serving ages 5-17, and 'longest running youth sports league in Western Canada' — matching the 'bills itself' framing exactly. rcba.ca and leagues.teamlinkt.com/rcba are the same TeamLinkt-powered site; https://app.teamlinkt.com/register/find/rcba verified live (HTTP 200). Independent corroboration of the org: BSI member registry lists 'Regina Community Basketball Association (RCBA)' under House Leagues - Youth (https://www.basketballsask.com/member-organizations/currentmembers); SkillShark case study documents ~1,200-kid registration scale (https://skillshark.com/case-studies/regina-community-basketball-association/). Caveat: the 1931 founding date traces only to RCBA's own history; nothing found contradicts it.

verification.claim: RYCBL is an independent league for club teams, registered member of Basketball Saskatchewan; multiple divisions of club (rep) teams; no published team count; software TeamLinkt (leagues.teamlinkt.com/rycbl) plus WordPress front site reginayouthclubbasketball.com with currently-expired SSL cert
verification.evidence: League site (https://reginayouthclubbasketball.com/) describes a league for club basketball teams Grades 3-8 plus a High School division, Nov-March, 'registered with Basketball Saskatchewan'. BSI membership independently corroborated: 'Regina Youth Club Basketball League' appears in the BSI current-members registry (https://www.basketballsask.com/member-organizations/currentmembers, categorized under House Leagues - Youth). TeamLinkt league platform live at https://leagues.teamlinkt.com/rycbl/Scores. Technical checks performed directly (curl, 2026-07-14): reginayouthclubbasketball.com serves 'curl: (60) SSL certificate problem: certificate has expired' — expired-cert claim CONFIRMED; page HTML contains multiple wp-content references — WordPress CONFIRMED. No team count published.

verification.claim: LSIBL is operated by Aboriginal Friendship Centres of Saskatchewan (AFCS) in partnership with One Love Basketball Inc. with Government of Canada funding; up to 650 athletes per season; 1,700+ cumulative registrants; 3,500+ athletes served per AFCS/Jumpstart materials; 44-88 coaches and 22+ officials certified; software TeamLinkt (separate Junior/Senior registration links); site on Wix (livingskiesbasketball.com)
verification.evidence: Operator/partnership/funding: AFCS initiative page (https://afcs.ca/initiative/living-skies-basketball-league/) confirms One Love Basketball partnership; Global News (https://globalnews.ca/news/7820724/living-skies-indigenous-basketball-league-fall/) and Canada Basketball (https://www.basketball.ca/news/living-skies-indigenous-basketball-leagues-becomes-the-latest-canada-basketball-unified-partner) independently confirm AFCS + One Love Basketball Inc. + Government of Canada support. 'Up to 650 athletes' — AFCS page and 2021 launch coverage verbatim. '1,700+ registrants' — Canada Basketball article (https://www.basketball.ca/news/living-skies-indigenous-basketball-league-leading-the-way-in-saskatchewan) states the league 'exceeded more than 1,700 registrants'. 3,500+ athletes: basketball.ca UNIFIED article ('empowered more than 3,500 athletes') and Eagle Feather News Jan 2026 (https://eaglefeathernews.com/2026/01/21/indigenous-basketball-league-supports-thousands-of-youth/, cites 3,800+ since 2021). '44-88+ coaches and 22+ officials' certification — exact text on AFCS page. TeamLinkt: livingskiesbasketball.com HTML contains multiple distinct app.teamlinkt.com/register links (register/find/livingskiesindigenousbasketball cid=48619 and cid=61614, plus register/go/.../20550), and the site has separate JUNIOR DIVISION and SENIOR DIVISION pages. Wix: confirmed directly via HTTP headers/HTML (wixstatic.com, parastorage, X-Wix headers, server: Pepyaka). Minor note: Global News Dec 2021 reported 500+ registered that season, and basketball.ca dates founding to 2020 (pandemic camps) with league play from 2021 — consistent, no contradiction.

verification.claim: HoopLife League (Regina fall/winter) is operated by HoopLife Basketball, a private academy/business ('The #1 Basketball Program in Regina') with its own facility HoopLife Centre at 661 Solomon Crescent, Regina; also operates in Saskatoon; multiple grade/gender divisions at a single dedicated facility; no published team count; registrations via Citrus Camps (register.citruscamps.com); marketing site custom/Webflow-style
verification.evidence: https://www.hooplifebasketball.com/regina/home carries the exact tagline 'HoopLife® | The #1 Basketball Program In Regina' and lists HoopLife Centre at 661 Solomon Cres, Regina SK S4N 6H9 (facility detail page https://www.hooplifebasketball.com/regina/facility). HoopLife League runs Grades 1-8, Oct-April, 9 competitive grade/gender divisions, all games at HoopLife Centre, paid NCCP-trained coaches and $750 fees (commercial operation). Saskatoon operations confirmed: https://www.hooplifebasketball.com/saskatoon/home ('The #1 Basketball Program In Saskatoon'). Citrus Camps confirmed: all Register buttons resolve to register.citruscamps.com (e.g. https://register.citruscamps.com/pg/pg_G7qJ0U7dKGC0Pqqh?type=camp; live tryout event pages found on the same portal). Independent corroboration of the league's existence: BSI member registry lists 'HoopLife Basketball (Regina)' under House Leagues - Youth (https://www.basketballsask.com/member-organizations/currentmembers).
verification.correction: One sub-detail unverified: whether the marketing site is Webflow-built was not confirmed (low-stakes; site is a polished custom marketing site either way). Also note BSI classifies HoopLife's Regina league as a youth house league, not an academy/club — the operator is a private business but the league sits in BSI's house-league category.

verification.claim: HoopLife Spring League (Regina) is operated by the same private HoopLife Basketball academy; 8 divisions; no published team count; software Citrus Camps (register.citruscamps.com)
verification.evidence: https://www.hooplifebasketball.com/regina/leagues/spring-league states verbatim '8 divisions for Boys and Girls' (Boys Gr 1/2, 3/4, 5/6 A&B, 7/8 A&B; Girls Gr 3/4, 5/6, 7/8), running April-June at the HoopLife Centre, same operator as the fall/winter league. Registration flows through Citrus Camps (register.citruscamps.com portal pg_G7qJ0U7dKGC0Pqqh) followed by evaluation camps and staff-balanced team placement. No team count published.

verification.claim: Moose Jaw Minor Basketball League is operated by Golden Ticket Sports (private operator; co-owner Tanner Brightman) at Golden Ticket Sports Centre, 1599 Main St N, Moose Jaw, after the volunteer Moose Jaw Minor Basketball Association disbanded in 2023; city-wide minor league; no published player count; software LeagueApps (sportscentre.leagueapps.com 'Moose Jaw Minor Basketball - Fall 2025'); TeamLinkt links used for 2023 takeover-season registration; tournaments via bracketteam.com
verification.evidence: MJMBA disbanding (Feb 2023) and Golden Ticket Sports takeover confirmed by two independent outlets: https://www.discovermoosejaw.com/articles/moose-jaw-minor-basketball-association-disbands-golden-ticket-sports-planning-fall-league and https://www.moosejawtoday.com/local-news/minor-b-ball-association-closes-new-league-to-start-next-fall-6554021. Tanner Brightman quoted as Golden Ticket Sports co-owner (co-founded with Chris King per MooseJawToday). Venue 1599 Main St N and K-Gr8 Sunday league per https://discovermoosejaw.com/articles/golden-ticket-sports-running-minor-basket-league-this-fall--winter. LeagueApps confirmed live: https://sportscentre.leagueapps.com/camps/4684965-moose-jaw-minor-basketball---fall-2025, plus multiple leagueapps.com links in goldenticketsports.com and goldenticketsportscentre.com HTML (checked directly). TeamLinkt in the 2023 takeover season confirmed: the DiscoverMooseJaw fall-league article embeds athlete registration https://app.teamlinkt.com/register/go/goldenticketsports/37606 and coach registration .../37616.
verification.correction: One sub-detail not corroborated: no evidence found that Golden Ticket tournaments run on bracketteam.com (their 2026 tournament listings on basketballsask.com and basketballmanitoba.ca use direct email contact; company sites reference LeagueApps). Treat the bracketteam.com note as unverified until a source is found.

verification.claim: PACBA Winter League is operated by Prince Albert Community Basketball Association — non-profit established 2009, listed in the BSI registry as 'Prince Albert Community Basketball League'; city league in its second relaunched season per paNOW Sept 2024; software RAMP InterActive (pacommunitybasketball.com, registration on RAMP)
verification.evidence: Independent news: paNOW, Sept 1 2024 (https://panow.com/2024/09/01/youth-basketball-league-returning-for-second-season/) confirms PACBA is a non-profit established 2009, winter league Oct-Feb with 11U/13U/15U boys and girls divisions, returning for its second season. BSI registry independently lists 'Prince Albert Community Basketball League' under House Leagues - Youth (https://www.basketballsask.com/member-organizations/currentmembers) — matching the claim's naming note exactly. RAMP confirmed: https://pacommunitybasketball.com/ page title is 'Prince Albert Community Basketball Association : Website by RAMP InterActive', and PACBA's AGM minutes are hosted on RAMP's cloud (https://cloud.rampinteractive.com/PrinceAlbertCommunityBasketball/files/...AGM...Nov%202,%202024...pdf), consistent with registration running on RAMP.

verification.claim: Saskatoon SLAM Basketball exists and its teams play in BSI Club Championships (Basketball Saskatchewan Provincials) and the BSI-sanctioned 2026 spring club tournament circuit
verification.evidence: Existence: https://saskatoonslam.com/ ('registered non-profit basketball club in Saskatoon' with U11-U18 boys/girls programs). Independent corroboration of BSI club status: the Basketball Saskatchewan current-members registry lists many SLAM entries under the Clubs category (Saskatoon Slam Storm/Magic/Suns/Phantoms, Slam Hanson, Slam Heat, Slam Swarm 14U Girls, etc.) — https://www.basketballsask.com/member-organizations/currentmembers. BSI describes the Club Championships (May 22-24 2026, Regina; 11U/13U/15U/Open HS) as the annual tournament open to BSI member clubs (https://www.basketballsask.com/players/tournaments). Spring circuit engagement: SLAM hosts the Prairie Season Opener Tournament April 24-26 2026 (https://saskatoonslam.com/tournament/), whose page contains multiple 'BSI' references (sanctioning/membership language, verified in page HTML). Caveat: BSI does not publish Club Championships entry lists, so a specific SLAM entry in the 2026 event is inferred from registered-club status rather than a published bracket.

verification.claim: Greenwave United (Saskatoon Basketball Academy) exists and its teams play in BSI Club Championships and the BSI-sanctioned 2026 spring club tournament circuit
verification.evidence: Existence: https://saskatoonbasketball.com/greenwave-united (non-profit developmental club, U10-U17 plus HS/college, based at 2813 20th Street, Saskatoon; contact Sean Tyson). BSI club status independently corroborated: 'Greenwave United(Saskatoon)' listed under Clubs in the BSI registry (https://www.basketballsask.com/member-organizations/currentmembers), making it eligible for the Club Championships, which BSI runs for member clubs (https://www.basketballsask.com/players/tournaments). Saskatoon Basketball Academy link: Greenwave lives on the saskatoonbasketball.com domain (titled 'The Saskatoon Basketball Academy offers...' on /what-we-do), Sean Tyson is simultaneously Head Coach/Director of Basketball Operations at Saskatoon Basketball Academy and Technical Director of Greenwave United (https://www.proleveltraining.com/sean-tyson, LinkedIn), and the site carries an 'SBA Alumni' section.
verification.correction: Precision note for the pipeline: Greenwave United and Saskatoon Basketball Academy are affiliated sister programs under the same leadership and domain (SBA ran an NPA prep team in 2018; Greenwave is the club arm) — record them as affiliated rather than one org renamed. Club Championships participation is inferred from BSI registered-club status; no published entry list.

verification.claim: SaskHoops Elite Basketball (Saskatoon) exists and its teams play in BSI Club Championships and the BSI-sanctioned 2026 spring club tournament circuit
verification.evidence: Existence: Facebook presence (https://www.facebook.com/SaskHoopsElite/ — 'SaskHoops Elite Basketball, Saskatoon SK'). Independent corroboration: 'SaskHoops Elite(Saskatoon)' is listed under the Clubs category in the Basketball Saskatchewan current-members registry (https://www.basketballsask.com/member-organizations/currentmembers); the BSI Club Championships (May 22-24 2026, Regina) is the annual event open to BSI member clubs and the listed May 2026 tournaments (May Madness, Misfits Invitational, Battle in The Bridge City, etc.) form the BSI-sanctioned spring circuit those clubs play (https://www.basketballsask.com/players/tournaments). Caveat: participation in the specific 2026 championships/tournaments is inferred from current BSI club registration; BSI publishes no entry lists.

verification.claim: Houndsville Hoops (Saskatoon) exists and its teams play in BSI Club Championships
verification.evidence: Existence: 'Houndsville Hoops(Saskatoon)' appears under the Clubs category in the Basketball Saskatchewan current-members registry (https://www.basketballsask.com/member-organizations/currentmembers) — an independent, PSO-maintained source — plus an active Instagram (https://www.instagram.com/houndsvillehoops/). The BSI Club Championships is the annual tournament open to BSI member clubs (https://www.basketballsask.com/players/tournaments). Caveat: no published entry list or results naming Houndsville at a specific championships; the play-in claim rests on its current registered-club status with BSI (which exists precisely to enter BSI club competition). Club has a thin public footprint — no standalone website found.

verification.claim: Stutter Steps Basketball (Saskatoon) exists and its teams play in BSI Club Championships
verification.evidence: Existence: 'Stutter Steps(Saskatoon)' appears under the Clubs category in the Basketball Saskatchewan current-members registry (https://www.basketballsask.com/member-organizations/currentmembers), plus active social presence in Saskatoon (https://www.facebook.com/stuttersteps.bball/, https://www.instagram.com/stuttersteps.bball/). BSI Club Championships is open to BSI member clubs (https://www.basketballsask.com/players/tournaments). Same caveat as Houndsville: participation inferred from registered-club status; no published entry list; no standalone website found.

verification.claim: YXE Elite Basketball Club (Saskatoon) exists; leagues/competitions it plays in unknown
verification.evidence: Existence independently corroborated: 'YXE Elite' is listed under the Clubs category in the Basketball Saskatchewan current-members registry (https://www.basketballsask.com/member-organizations/currentmembers). Little other public footprint found in this pass (no website surfaced).
verification.correction: The '?' can be partially filled: as a BSI-registered member club, YXE Elite is registered for BSI-sanctioned club competition, whose flagship is the BSI Club Championships (May 22-24 2026, Regina) — same inference basis as the other Saskatoon clubs, though no direct entry evidence exists.

pso.name: Basketball Nova Scotia (BNS)
pso.address: 5516 Spring Garden Road, 4th Floor, Halifax, NS B3J 1G6
pso.software: RAMP InterActive (website 'Website by RAMP InterActive'; RAMP mirror basketballnovascotia.msa4.rampinteractive.com; registration via basketballnsclub.rampregistrations.com; RAMP officials-assigning). LEGACY: GOALLINE still online for provincials (bnsprovincials.goalline.ca).
pso.notes: NOTE: basketball.ns.ca does NOT resolve — current domain is basketballnovascotia.ca. Member-club registry lists ~45 affiliated clubs by region with president names+emails: Cape Breton 6 (Basketball Cape Breton, Cape Breton Dream Girls, Margaree Mountain Lions, Northside Youth BA, PUSH Cape Breton, Inverness Minor), Central 19 (Bedford Minor, Cole Harbour Minor, Community YMCA, Dartmouth Celtics, Dartmouth Lakers, East Preston Pacers, Express Basketball Academy, Fall River Rebels, Halifax Hurricanes, Halifax Wheelchair BC, etc.), Fundy 8 (Amherst, Colchester, Cumberland Crushers, East Hants, etc.), Highlands 3 (Antigonish Minor, Pictou County Raptors, Pictou County Lightning), South Shore 4 (Shelburne County BA, South Shore Surf, Yarmouth County Minor, Queens County Bears), Valley 5 (Axe Basketball, Annapolis County Athletics, Kingston Greenwood, Kings Minor, Shooting Star). Membership seasons: Fall/Winter Sep 1–Apr 30, Spring/Summer May 1–Aug 31. Membership contact: Emily Lerette, Manager Membership & Events, bnsoperations@sportnovascotia.ca (BNS sits within Sport Nova Scotia building). Runs: Provincial Club Championships (U12/U14/U16-18), Scotia Showcase summer tournament, Jr. NBA youth program, J-PEP performance program, provincial teams, wheelchair basketball. Officials: Nova Scotia Basketball Officials (nsbo.ca); Metro Area Basketball Officials on GOALLINE (mabo.goalline.ca). EXCLUDED from league list per scope: NSSAF school sport / Metro High School Athletic Association (school varsity), Cape Breton Division 1 High School league (LeagueLineup, marked for removal/defunct), Eastern Canadian Basketball League ECBL (adult/semi-pro), Halifax Sport & Social Club + Brodie Rec (adult rec). Sources: https://www.basketballnovascotia.ca/ ; https://basketballnovascotia.ca/content/member-clubs ; http://basketballnovascotia.msa4.rampinteractive.com/content/member-club-registration ; http://bnsprovincials.goalline.ca/

leagues.name: Metro Basketball Association (MBA / Metro Basketball League)
leagues.operator: Metro Basketball Association — independent non-profit single league operator for HRM minor basketball (member clubs field teams; clubs handle registration/practices, MBA schedules all games)
leagues.level: metro (dominant winter club league for Halifax Regional Municipality, drawing clubs from beyond HRM: East Hants, South Shore)
leagues.ages_tiers: U10, U12, U14, U16, U18, boys & girls; 6+ tiered divisions per larger age group; U16/U18 girls combined into one age division
leagues.region: Halifax Regional Municipality + surrounding (Bedford, Dartmouth, Cole Harbour, Sackville, Fall River, St. Margaret's Bay, East Hants, South Shore)
leagues.scale: 350+ teams (published by member club East Hants Tigers); member clubs seen in league pages/BNS registry include Bedford Eagles, Cole Harbour Rockets, Sackville Storm, Halifax Hurricanes, Dartmouth Celtics, Dartmouth Lakers, East Hants Tigers, South Shore Surf, YMCA Panthers (Community Y), Tri City Vipers, St. Margaret's Bay SLAM, Fall River Rebels
leagues.season: Tryouts mid-September; league play late October through end of February with playoffs mid-February; one game per weekend (U10/U12 Saturdays, U14–U18 Sundays) at gyms across HRM
leagues.software: RAMP InterActive (mbans.ca is 'Website by RAMP InterActive'; RAMP host mbansca.msa4.rampinteractive.com; division/gamesheet/standings all RAMP). LEGACY: previously on itSportsNet (old 'Halifax Metro Basketball Association' league pages still indexed at itsportsnet.com leagueID=8439). Member clubs register players via their own RAMP registrations (e.g. East Hants Tigers rampregistrations.com) except YMCA (activecommunities.com)
leagues.contact: lerettcp@mbans.ca; PO Box 31133, Gladstone RPO, Halifax, NS B3K 5Y1 (email/address from a third-party scrape of mbans.ca — verify; club-contacts page at https://mbans.ca/content/club-contacts)
leagues.notes: THE anchor league of NS youth basketball — single operator, 350+ teams. mbans.ca was returning HTTP 500 throughout research (2026-07-14) though its pages are live-indexed; RAMP mirror returned 403 — data assembled from member-club pages and search-indexed league pages. Made news Dec 2023 for banning spectators over behaviour (globalnews.ca/news/10171626).

leagues.name: BNS Provincial Club Championships (U12 / U14 / U16-U18 Provincials)
leagues.operator: Basketball Nova Scotia (PSO)
leagues.level: provincial (annual championship tournaments for BNS member-club teams)
leagues.ages_tiers: U12, U14, U16/U18, boys & girls, tiered into divisions via 3 required seeding games
leagues.region: Nova Scotia (host regions rotate; announced per age group)
leagues.scale: Open to all ~45 BNS member clubs' teams; participant counts not published
leagues.season: Spring: 2026 dates — U12 Mar 7-8, U14 Mar 28-29, U16/U18 Apr 11-12
leagues.software: RAMP Registration (basketballnsclub.rampregistrations.com; teams must be registered in their club's RAMP system). LEGACY: GOALLINE provincials site (bnsprovincials.goalline.ca) still online with prior years' schedules
leagues.notes: The competitive endpoint for club teams outside metro — e.g. Colchester BA states all its teams participate in BNS club Provincials. GOALLINE→RAMP migration is competitive intel: BNS consolidated onto RAMP.

leagues.name: Scotia Showcase Tournament
leagues.operator: Basketball Nova Scotia (PSO)
leagues.ages_tiers: U14, U15, U17 boys & girls + Wheelchair divisions (2025 edition)
leagues.season: Summer — July 4-6, 2025; 2026 edition scheduled with schedule published on BNS RAMP site
leagues.software: RAMP InterActive (event pages on basketballnovascotia RAMP site)
leagues.notes: PSO's flagship spring/summer-season event anchoring the May 1–Aug 31 BNS summer membership season.

leagues.name: Atlantic Elite Basketball League (AEBL)
leagues.operator: Atlantic Elite Basketball League (independent operator, info@aebl.ca; positions itself as elite talent-exposure circuit for university/college scouting)
leagues.level: regional elite circuit (Atlantic Canada, session-based travel league; NS clubs compete)
leagues.ages_tiers: Winter: U8–U14 boys & girls; Summer: U8–U18 boys & girls
leagues.region: Atlantic Canada (rotating host communities); NS participant confirmed: South Shore Lightning Basketball Association
leagues.scale: Team counts not published (league launched recently — social accounts created ~2025)
leagues.season: Winter: November–April, 18 games in weekend 3-game sessions (one per month); Summer: May–August, 15 games in weekend 3-game sessions
leagues.software: Squarespace website with built-in commerce/registration cart; team registration at aebl.ca/registerateam
leagues.notes: New cross-provincial elite layer on top of the club system — runs both a winter season parallel to MBA and a summer season. Also runs Girls Showcase Events. Worth tracking as an emerging operator with no established league-management stack (Squarespace, not RAMP/TeamLinkt).

leagues.name: Basketball Cape Breton Summer League
leagues.operator: Basketball Cape Breton (non-profit regional development organization; BNS member; contact David Ogbuah, david.ogbuah@basketballcapebreton.com)
leagues.level: local (Cape Breton Regional Municipality summer youth league)
leagues.ages_tiers: Middle/high-school ages (BCB programming spans youth born 2012–2019 for camps; summer league for older youth)
leagues.season: Summer (July–August); 2026 camps July 6-17, tip-off sessions July 2-3
leagues.software: Google Forms for all registrations (no league-management platform); web presence is a Linktree (basketballcapebreton.com 301s to linktr.ee/basketballcb)
leagues.notes: Cape Breton has NO standing winter club league found — clubs (Northside Youth BA, Inverness Minor, Margaree Mountain Lions, Dream Girls) run programs and travel to tournaments/provincials. BCB also stood up an ad-hoc juvenile league in 2017 when teacher work-to-rule cancelled school ball (CTV). Zero-software greenfield: entire region runs on Google Forms.

leagues.name: PUSH Cape Breton (in-house game programming + Canada Day Classic)
leagues.operator: PUSH Cape Breton (private academy/club, 'Preparation Until Success Happens'; Tyrone Levingston, info@pushcb.com; BNS member club)
leagues.level: local club-run circuit (training seasons with games + annual tournament; travel/elite teams U12–U18 compete externally)
leagues.ages_tiers: Training ages 6-16 all levels ($500/season, 3 sessions/week); Travel Teams U12/U14/U16/U18 ($250); invite-only Elite Teams 12-18
leagues.region: Cape Breton (New Waterford Community Centre Hub, PUSH Arena, C200; Glace Bay tournament)
leagues.season: Four training seasons (winter/spring/summer/fall; summer 2026 starts July 19); Canada Day Classic tournament July 4-6, 2025 (U12–U18 boys & girls)
leagues.software: Own website registration at pushcb.com/registration; tournaments hosted on Exposure Events (basketball.exposureevents.com/organizations/33899)
leagues.notes: Private academy type (rare in NS — most clubs are community associations). Not a standing league but the main non-BCB game provider on the island; Exposure Events usage is the competitive-intel data point.

leagues.name: Nova Scotia Valour League (All Canada Basketball)
leagues.operator: All Canada Basketball (Ontario-based commercial operator, tricity40basketball.com; contact iqahwash@allcanadabasketball.com)
leagues.level: spring-summer circuit (commercial pay-to-play league; individual + team entry)
leagues.ages_tiers: Novice (2011-12), Atom (2009-10), Bantam (2007-08), Major (2005-06) — boys & girls, teams of 10-12
leagues.region: Halifax (Ummah Community Centre, 2510 Matthias St, Halifax B3L 0A9)
leagues.season: 8 Saturdays, May 8 – June 26 (page dated 2021); $250+HST per player incl. adidas uniform
leagues.software: All Canada Basketball's own site registration (WordPress); no league-management platform identified
leagues.notes: Page content is from 2021 and birth years were never updated — current operation in NS unconfirmed; operator's active Valour Leagues appear to be Kitchener-Waterloo. Treat as possibly defunct NS expansion attempt; verify before outreach.

leagues.name: National Preparatory Association (NPA) — NS entrant: Halifax Prep

verification.claim: Metro Basketball Association (MBA/Metro Basketball League) — independent non-profit single league operator for HRM minor basketball (clubs handle registration/practices, MBA schedules games); 350+ teams; member clubs incl. Bedford Eagles, Cole Harbour Rockets, Sackville Storm, Halifax Hurricanes, Dartmouth Celtics, Dartmouth Lakers, East Hants Tigers, South Shore Surf, YMCA Panthers, Tri City Vipers, St. Margaret's Bay SLAM, Fall River Rebels; software RAMP InterActive (mbans.ca, host mbansca.msa4.rampinteractive.com); legacy itSportsNet leagueID=8439; clubs run own RAMP registrations
verification.evidence: 350+ teams independently published by member club East Hants Tigers: https://tricountytigers.ca/content/metro-basketball-league-u10-u18 ('over 350 teams participate in the Metro League'). All 12 named clubs verified on MBA RAMP pages or club sites: Bedford Eagles https://mbans.ca/team/4532/922/10347/116932 ; Cole Harbour Rockets https://www.mbans.ca/team/4532/920/10343/116949 ; Dartmouth Celtics https://mbans.ca/team/9267/0/10346/211049/games ; Dartmouth Lakers https://mbans.ca/team/9267/922/10347/211471 ; Fall River Rebels https://www.mbans.ca/team/4532/923/10350/119511/player/1392362 ; Tri City Vipers https://fallriverrebels.com/team/10998/922/10348/274027 ; Halifax Hurricanes https://www.hhbball.com/content/u10-u18-minor-basketball-teams ('Our U10 to U18 boys and girls teams play in the Metro Basketball Association'; club handles assessments/registration/practice scheduling — corroborates division of labour); Sackville Storm, South Shore Surf, St. Margarets Bay SLAM, YMCA Panthers, Express listed as MBA teams on mbans.ca. Own registration page: https://pushcb.com/registration. Exposure Events org 33899 confirmed: https://basketball.exposureevents.com/organizations/33899/push-cape-breton (hosts Canada Day Classic, Glace Bay NS, U12-U18 boys/girls). BNS member confirmed on PSO registry: https://basketballnovascotia.ca/content/member-clubs (PUSH Cape Breton, Cape Breton region).
verification.correction: info@pushcb.com email not independently re-verified; everything else checks out.

verification.claim: Nova Scotia Valour League operated by All Canada Basketball (Ontario-based commercial operator, tricity40basketball.com; iqahwash@allcanadabasketball.com); own WordPress site registration, no league-management platform
verification.evidence: Operator's own site confirms all details: https://tricity40basketball.com/nova-scotia-valour-league/ (house league, all skill levels Novice/Atom/Bantam/Major, at Ummah Community Centre Halifax; contact iqahwash@allcanadabasketball.com; WordPress site with own 'Register Today!' flow, no external league-management platform). Ontario base corroborated by its Kitchener-Waterloo Valour League https://tricity40basketball.com/valourleague/. However, NO independent source (news, BNS, venue, or club site) attesting the NS league's operation was found — existence rests solely on the operator's own site, so this stays at plausible rather than confirmed.

verification.claim: NPA (pan-Canadian prep circuit) — NS entrant Halifax Prep, private academy founded by Ming Folahan; province's only NPA member per CBC 2021
verification.evidence: NPA membership confirmed on league site: https://www.npacanada.com/page/show/5234946-halifax-prep. 'Province's only member of the National Preparatory Association (NPA), a pan-Canadian league' per CBC (2021): https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/nova-scotia/halifax-basketball-club-vandalism-struggles-for-acceptance-1.5973342. Founder Naofall 'Ming' Folahan corroborated by Wagner College magazine https://wagner.edu/wagnermagazine/mings-next-chapter/ and https://signalhfx.ca/how-a-high-schooler-with-little-basketball-training-stands-out/ ($5,000/yr program, national travel schedule, scouting-service exposure).
verification.correction: The 'only NPA member' fact is as-of-2021 (CBC); current NPA membership should be re-checked against npacanada.com before use in 2026 pipeline data.

pso.name: Basketball New Brunswick (BNB) / Basketball Nouveau-Brunswick
pso.address: 900 Hanwell Rd, Unit 31, Fredericton, NB E3B 6A2
pso.software: RAMP InterActive end-to-end: RAMP CMS website (basketball.nb.ca), RAMP Registration (rampregistrations.com) mandated for ALL member clubs/rep coaches/team registrations (incl. police checks 'done directly through the RAMP system'); supplemented by Google Forms/Sheets for score reporting, Looking-for-Games listings, tournament listings and hosting applications; coach clinics via gameplanbasketball.ca; officials via NBAABO (nbaabo.homestead.com) with per-zone email assigners
pso.notes: Staff: Executive Director Alyssa Lebans (ed@basketball.nb.ca), Director of Operations Ryan Chase (ryan.chase@basketball.nb.ca). Registry lists 38 member groups for 2025-26 (as of 13 Apr 2026) across 4 regions - Central/West (14, incl. Junior Reds, Reds Selects, Fredericton Fusion/FYBA, YFC Elite, Woodstock, Grand Falls, Carleton North, Eagles Basketball Academy [private]), East/Greater Moncton (11, incl. 506 Elite [private facility club], Basketball Dieppe [francophone], Moncton BA, Riverview, Salisbury-Petitcodiac, Sackville, Fundy Albert), North (3: Miramichi River Hoops, Acadie Basketball [francophone], Bathurst BA), South/Saint John (12, incl. Lancaster, KVBA, East Saint John, Hampton, Sussex, Surge [for-profit], K's Port City, River Valley, St. Stephen, East Coast BA). Mix of community associations, rep clubs and private academies; several francophone (Dieppe, Acadie). NOTABLE WHITESPACE: no member club currently listed for Edmundston/Madawaska or Campbellton/Restigouche despite BNB having a referee assigner for Campbellton/Dalhousie zone. Season windows 2025-26: tryouts from early Sept, competitive practices Oct 6, games Oct 17, provincials Feb-Mar, sanctioned spring/summer window May 1-Jul 31. Game caps before provincials: U11 20, U12/U13 26, U14 30. BNB also runs adult provincials + New Brunswick Mens League and there is a separate Moncton Women's Basketball League and Maritime Women's Basketball Association (all adult - excluded from this census). GTM intel: province is a single-vendor RAMP shop at PSO level; club level fragments into RAMP (MBA, RMBA, Fusion, 506 Elite, YFC), TeamLinkt (Miramichi, Fusion tournaments), TeamSnap (KVBA, Lancaster, Hampton), GoDaddy/WordPress DIY (ESJMBA, Dieppe, Lancaster, Surge) and dead legacy Pointstreak/itSportsNet/Goalline/SportsEngine instances (MKMBA, Miramichi, Hampton). Sources: https://www.basketball.nb.ca/content/contact-us | https://www.basketball.nb.ca/content/find-a-club-association | https://cloud.rampinteractive.com/basketballnewbrunswick/files/Coaches%20Handbook%202024-25.pdf

leagues.name: BNB Provincial League (U12-U14)
leagues.operator: Basketball New Brunswick
leagues.ages_tiers: U12, U13, U14 boys & girls; tiered divisions (A/B/C/D observed for 2025-26, e.g. U14 Boys A-D)
leagues.scale: Teams from ~20+ member clubs observed in indexed 2025-26 division listings (Fusion, KV Slammers, River Hoops, Southern Victoria Vikings, Woodstock Thunder, 506 Elite, Sussex, Lancaster Lynx, Surge Spartans, Grand Falls Knights, Sackville Titans, St. Stephen, Carleton North, Bathurst Blazers, Dieppe, Miramichi, MBA Hawks, RMBA Royals, Salisbury etc.); exact team count not published
leagues.season: Oct-Feb (2025-26: practices from Oct 6, games from Oct 17; no-game weekend early Feb; league teams auto-entered into Feb provincials). Historically (2024-25 handbook) rep season ran on booked games via zone assigners + BNB Looking-for-Games sheet with scores reported to BNB for seeding; 2025-26 formalized as registered 'BNB Provincial League' divisions
leagues.software: RAMP (team + participant registration via rampregistrations.com; league content on RAMP CMS site); Google Forms score reporting; Google Sheets game-finding
leagues.contact: info@basketball.nb.ca; Ryan Chase ryan.chase@basketball.nb.ca (Director of Operations)
leagues.notes: Quote from BNB provincials page: 'ONLY TEAMS NOT already registered for the BNB Provincial League have to register, teams registered for League play are automatically entered into Provincial Championships.' FIBA rules w/ BNB fair-play minimums (8 min/player U13-14), no half-court zone U13/14, no zone at all U12. This is THE provincial winter rep competition all club travel teams feed into.

pso.name: Newfoundland and Labrador Basketball Association (NLBA)
pso.software: RAMP Registrations for all individual membership + tournament registration ($21.50/person Canada Basketball + NLBA fee via NLBA.rampregistrations.com). PSO website is a Duda-platform site built by Mile One Digital (irp.cdn-website.com assets); league schedules/standings published as static PDFs/images, no interactive scheduling/stats platform. Scores site nlscoreboard.com referenced for tournaments (SSL cert expired as of Jul 2026).
pso.notes: PSO since 1964; note nlba.ca does NOT resolve — current domain is newfoundlandlabradorbasketball.com. Staff: Judy Byrne ED (jbyrne@sportnl.ca), Amanda Miles (nlbainfo@sportnl.ca), Laura Chatman (lchatman@sportnl.ca), Kieran Lynch (klynch@sportnl.ca), Alicia Bird (abird@sportnl.ca). Member registry = two pages: /minor-clubs (find-a-club directory) and /membership (RAMP-linked club list). 17 minor club members (2025-26): 709 Basketball (Outer Cove/Avalon/Southern Shore-Goulds branches), CBN Youth Ball, CE23 Academy (private academy, Carl English, own facility 1260 Kenmount Rd Paradise), Central Female Basketball, Central NL Minor/Youth Basketball (Grand Falls-Windsor, bcookegfw@hotmail.com 709-486-3136), Conception Bay Seeds, Fogo Central Academy, Grenfell Jr Warriors (Corner Brook, MUN Grenfell campus), Labrador West Miner Basketball, Mount Pearl Minor Basketball (community assoc, K-Gr9, GOALLINE site mountpearlbasketball.com unreachable Jul 2026, mp.minor.bb@gmail.com), NLBT Next Level Basketball Training (private, nlbt.ca, info@nlbt.ca 709-690-5255), NLBA Programs, Paradise Minor Basketball (community, K-Gr8, paradiseminorbasketball.ca), Rain City Athletics (private academy est. 2023, St. John's, raincitynl.com), River Ratz Basketball (CBN, run by Aspire Rec, Aspire.Rec@outlook.com), Riverside Raptors, St. John's Minor Basketball (largest community club, K-Gr12, sjmb.ca). Find-a-club also lists: Court College Basketball (private, Northpoint Sports, john@northpointsports.ca), FORCE Basketball (Gander, forcebasketballnl@gmail.com), Goulds Basketball (gouldsbasketball.ca, info@709basketball.com), Central Wolves (centralwolves@hotmail.com), Western Avalanche Basketball Club (western NL, wabcavalanche@gmail.com), Corner Brook Shooting Stars (facebook.com/CornerBrookMinorBasketball). KEY GTM INTEL: Rock Sports Group Inc. (rocksports.ca, jon@rocksports.ca) is a private operator administering SJMB, Paradise Minor, Gander Minor, CBN Minor, Torbay Minor, Future Hawks and invite-only Rock Elite — all registering on Citrus (register.citruscamps.com). Avalon/709 Basketball moved from Citrus (fall 2024) to TeamLinkt (app.teamlinkt.com, portal avalonbasketball.com/current). Excluded per scope: NLBA Provincial School Tournaments Gr4-11, December Tip-Off (Gr7-8) and Hall of Fame Cup Elite-8 (Gr9-12) — school-team events; School Sports NL; adult senior members (Metro Ladies BL, Metro Men's BL, St. John's Div 1 Ladies, GFW Donnini's, Pinoy Gander, Filipino Newfoundlanders Assoc; St. John's Amateur Basketball Assoc runs on TeamLinkt). Jr. NBA program (Gr1-4, no games, RAMP-registered, jrnba.ca directory) is grassroots skills only. Source URLs: https://www.newfoundlandlabradorbasketball.com/membership · https://www.newfoundlandlabradorbasketball.com/minor-clubs · https://www.newfoundlandlabradorbasketball.com/nlba-tournaments · https://www.newfoundlandlabradorbasketball.com/jr-nba · https://rocksports.ca/basketball/ · https://sportnl.ca/member-sports/newfoundland-and-labrador-basketball-association/

pso.name: Basketball PEI (PEI Amateur Basketball Association)
pso.email: info@basketballpei.ca (Executive/Technical Director: Josh Whitty, josh@basketballpei.ca; President: Sean Seviour, president@basketballpei.ca; Island Aces club program: islandaces@gmail.com)
pso.address: Room 205, 40 Enman Crescent, Charlottetown, PE C1E 1E6 (current site footer prints C1A 1E6; 211 PEI and pre-2020 site show C1E 1E6)
pso.software: Website: Web.com SiteBuilder (mywebsitebuilder.com — fully JS-rendered). League scheduling/results: GameOn Mobile app (app.gameonmobile.com, org 4689; 'PEI Mini Rep Leagues' = league 4194). Registrations: Google Forms (Island Aces ID camps, SuperSkillz camps, spring leagues, coach clinics; payment by cash/cheque/e-transfer to info@basketballpei.ca). Master event schedule: shared Google Sheet. Legacy: WordPress site pre-2020; inactive SportsEngine directory listing. NO RAMP/TeamLinkt/TeamSnap observed anywhere in the PSO stack, despite Canada Basketball's national RAMP member-management partnership — an extremely light stack and a soft target.
pso.notes: Member registry = 11 community minor basketball associations (all volunteer community associations; NO private academies or independent rep clubs found on PEI): Charlottetown Tigers MBA (contact Jeff Perry; legacy GOALLINE site tigersbasketball.ca offline; FB facebook.com/CharlottetownTigersBball; email tigersbasketball.ca@gmail.com per SportsEngine listing), Cornwall Comets MBA (Eric Saindon), Stratford Foxes MBA (Jason MacDonald; stratfordfoxes.ca offline), Morell Wildcats MBA (Jody Kinney), North Shore Ospreys MBA (Duane Pineau & Lucas Squires; Pointstreak site ospreysminorbasketball.pointstreaksites.com; serves Gulf Shore/Central Queens/St-Augustin schools, grades 1-6), South Shore Sharks MBA (contact VACANT), Kensington Nor'easters MBA (Stacey Kember), East Prince Sting MBA in Summerside (Jennifer Halupa; legacy stingbasketball.ca offline; FB EastPrinceStingBasketball), West Prince Wolverines MBA (Joey Smallman), Three Rivers MBA in Montague area (Shawn Fraser & Duane Yoshikawa), Souris Slammers MBA (John MacIntosh). Contact names published on the registry page; personal emails are NOT published. The PSO itself runs the only rep 'club' above Mini age: Island Aces Basketball Club (U11-U14 boys/girls teams, ID camps $25, competes regionally vs NS/NB clubs) plus Junior Aces (U10), High Performance Programs (Women's gr 5-12, Junior Boys gr 5-9, Senior Boys gr 10-12 w/ UPEI; $200 incl. BPEI membership), U15/U17 provincial teams, and SuperSkillz camps (sponsor Courtside Sneakers). Grade 7-12 in-season competition runs through PEISAA school basketball (GOALLINE-powered peisaa.pe.ca) — excluded from this census as school-based. Adult 'Courtside Sneakers Senior Men's League' listed as coming soon (excluded, adult-only). Board includes Minor Basketball Rep Clint Brink. Sources: https://basketballpei.ca/about-bpei, https://basketballpei.ca/minor-associations, https://basketballpei.ca/island-aces, https://basketballpei.ca/high-performance, https://basketballpei.ca/senior-leagues, https://pe.211.ca/result/basketball-programs-63745789, http://web.archive.org/web/20190818125158/http://basketballpei.ca/2019-2020-minor-basketball-registration-details/

leagues.name: PEI Mini Rep League
leagues.operator: Basketball PEI (PSO-run; teams entered by member minor associations)
leagues.ages_tiers: Mini = grades 5-6; four divisions: Mini Division I Boys, Division I Girls, Division II Boys, Division II Girls; culminates in BPEI-run Mini Provincial Championships
leagues.scale: Teams drawn from the 11 member minor basketball associations; per-division team counts not published. BPEI supplies referees for regular season and everything (officials, gyms, minor officials) for Provincials; host association books gyms in season.
leagues.season: School-year season (association registration each September; 2023-24 schedule published by BPEI; Provincials at season end)
leagues.software: Schedules/results hosted on GameOn Mobile (app.gameonmobile.com, organization 4689, league 4194 'PEI Mini Rep Leagues') — linked from BPEI's official Minor Programs page. Registration handled by each association (largely offline/Google Forms).
leagues.notes: The only standing club-team winter league on PEI. Above grade 6 the competitive pathway runs through PEISAA school basketball (excluded from this census) plus BPEI's Island Aces club and High Performance/provincial-team programs.

leagues.name: BPEI Jamboree Series + minor-association house leagues (Small Ball / Junior Mini / House-League Mini)
leagues.operator: The 11 BPEI member minor basketball associations, coordinated by Basketball PEI
leagues.ages_tiers: Small Ball (grades K-2), Junior Mini (grades 3-4), House League Mini (grades 5-6); Jr. NBA Youth Basketball curriculum (ages 5-12, 12-week program) delivered through the associations
leagues.region: Province-wide via 11 community associations (Charlottetown, Cornwall, Stratford, Summerside/East Prince, Kensington, North Shore, South Shore, Morell, Souris, Three Rivers/Montague, West Prince)
leagues.scale: 11 associations; each encouraged to host one Saturday jamboree per season for Small Ball/Jr Mini/House-League Mini teams; participation numbers not published
leagues.season: Fall-winter school-year season; jamborees on Saturdays ('2023-24 Jamboree Schedule' published by BPEI); association registration typically September
leagues.software: No central platform. Legacy association sites: Charlottetown Tigers on GOALLINE (tigersbasketball.ca, now offline; inactive SportsEngine directory listing remains), North Shore Ospreys on Pointstreak Sites (still resolving), Stratford Foxes and East Prince Sting legacy domains offline; most associations now operate via Facebook + manual/Google Forms registration
leagues.notes: House play + jamborees is the entry-level 'league' layer under the Mini Rep League. North Shore Ospreys legacy pricing ~$80-100/season incl. kit (historical). This layer is a prime greenfield software target: no modern registration/scheduling stack observed at any association.

leagues.name: Courtside Sneakers Junior & Senior Boys Spring Leagues
leagues.operator: Basketball PEI (title sponsor: Courtside Sneakers, Charlottetown sneaker shop)
leagues.ages_tiers: Junior League: players entering junior high in fall (currently grades 6-8); Senior League: players entering high school (currently grades 9-11)
leagues.scale: 4 teams per league (2024 edition), double round-robin plus playoffs, 8 game sessions; capped enrollment ('space is limited')
leagues.season: Mid-April to mid-May (2024: April 18 - May 14; Tuesdays 4-8pm, Thursdays 5-9pm)
leagues.software: Registration via Google Form; no league-management platform observed
leagues.notes: Cost $125/player incl. t-shirt (2024). One of the few post-school-season play opportunities on the Island.

leagues.name: Hopyard Spring League (girls counterpart to Courtside Sneakers leagues)
leagues.operator: Basketball PEI (sponsor: The Hopyard restaurant)
leagues.season: Spring (April-May window, paired with the Courtside Sneakers boys leagues)
leagues.software: Google Forms registration (consistent with BPEI's other spring leagues)
leagues.notes: Existence evidenced only by the official BPEI news title 'Hopyard/Courtside Sneakers Spring Leagues' (the news article body is not retrievable from the JS-rendered site; the Facebook post detailed only the boys leagues). Presumed the girls league; details unconfirmed.

leagues.name: Red Rock Classic (annual spring club tournament)
leagues.operator: Basketball PEI
leagues.ages_tiers: U11 Boys & Girls, U13 Boys & Girls, U14 Boys, U15 Boys & Girls, U16 Boys, U17 Boys & Girls (per most recent master schedule)
leagues.region: Charlottetown (UPEI, Holland College, Charlottetown Rural, Colonel Gray and other school gyms)
leagues.scale: ~10 divisions, roughly 4-8 teams each; draws NS/NB club programs (Fury, 506 Elite, Preston Elite, Metro Elite, Reds, UBB, Royals etc.) plus PEI's Island Aces and provincial teams
leagues.season: Late May annually (2026: May 22-24)
leagues.software: Schedule/results published as a shared Google Sheet linked from the BPEI events page
leagues.notes: Not a standing league but the anchor club-competition event on PEI and the main occasion Maritime club teams travel to the Island; included because it is where PEI club teams (Island Aces U11-U14) get league-style competition.

clubs.notes: The closest thing PEI has to an elite/AAU-style club — run by the PSO itself, not private. No private basketball academies were found anywhere in PEI (searches for academies/private training returned only PSO SuperSkillz camps and UPEI varsity camps, which are excluded).

pso.name: Basketball Yukon | Basketball NWT | Nunavut Basketball Association (NUBA) — one PSO per territory
pso.email: Basketball Yukon: admin@basketballyukon.ca | Basketball NWT: leo.bnwt@gmail.com (programs), bnwttd1@gmail.com (Cole Marshall, TD), dhealynwt@gmail.com (Damien Healy) | NUBA: info@nunavutbasketball.com, nunavutbasketball@outlook.com (ops manager Dave Penney; president Gary Kennedy)
pso.address: Basketball Yukon: 4061 4th Avenue, Whitehorse YT Y1A 1H1 | Basketball NWT: 4908 49 St, Yellowknife NT X1A 3X7 (Sport North building; per secondary listing — BNWT's own site is under construction) | NUBA: Iqaluit NU (no published street address)
pso.software: Basketball Yukon: WordPress/Divi site, no online member registry or registration platform found (clubs self-register; Wolf Pack uses Squarespace forms). Basketball NWT: RAMP InterActive website as of 2026 — MIGRATED from Stack Sports GOALLINE (bnwt.goalline.ca dead, old page.php URLs 404/500); a stale SportsEngine org page also exists; member clubs (Eagles, Ravens) register on RAMP Registrations. NUBA: Wix site; event registration via Trackie (trackie.com) through NU Sport Federation.
pso.notes: No territory publishes a member-club registry. Closest equivalents: Basketball Yukon's youth-programs page listing its two youth clubs (https://basketballyukon.ca/youth-programs/) and the NWT club set documented at https://cfmws.ca/yellowknife/basketball. Landscape reality: no standing club-vs-club youth leagues at scale — competitive play runs through school sport (excluded per scope: NTSSA Jr/Sr Cager, Nunavut Senior Cager at Inuksuk HS, Yukon school leagues), annual tournaments (Yukon Lights Out, NUBA regionals/territorials), and travel south. BNWT's RAMP site being 'under construction' (Jul 2026) = timing opportunity. EXCLUDED adult-only leagues found (competitive-intel software noted): Yellowknife Basketball Association ykba.com (WordPress), Yellowknife Philippine Basketball League, Whitehorse Women's BBall League, Yukon Men's BBall League, Filipino Canadian Basketball League of Yukon (~22 teams — TeamLinkt + LeagueLineup + HomeTeamsOnline), CFSAY (LeagueLineup), YPCBA (16 teams), Iqaluit Basketball League (RecLeague.net, archived 2019). Confidence-relevant caveat: BNWT Yellowknife address and the Inuvik/Hay River community-league contacts come from stale secondary directories — reconfirm before outreach. Whitehorse pro exhibition game (CEBL-style, 2024) indicates market appetite but is not youth infrastructure.

clubs.name: Nunavut Basketball Association (NUBA)
clubs.principal: Gary Kennedy — President (per Nunatsiaq News championship coverage); Abbas Parks — Qikiqtani representative, board of directors
clubs.programs: Territorial sport organization formed ~2021 and the sole youth basketball operator in Nunavut: grassroots camps/clinics (3-4 days each) in every region annually, Iqaluit youth clinics and 3-on-3 program, coaching education (Coaching 101), Basketball Day in Nunavut, Team Nunavut (first Canada Summer Games appearance 2025)
clubs.team_league_map: U19 Territorials held at Inuksuk High School, Iqaluit — 125 athletes from 7 communities (Pangnirtung, Arctic Bay, Cambridge Bay, Arviat, Baker Lake, Igloolik, Iqaluit), 3x3 format; Cambridge Bay swept 2024 titles (women's coach Daisy Dyer-Eyegetok). Communities field ad-hoc community teams, not formal clubs
clubs.software: Online application forms on own website; no commercial registration platform identified
clubs.type: territorial governing body (nonprofit) / direct program operator
clubs.notes: No standalone youth clubs or private academies found anywhere in Nunavut — basketball is organized entirely through NUBA plus school and community teams (Cambridge Bay is the talent hotbed, supplying ~half of Arctic Winter Games rosters). Iqaluit Basketball League on RecLeague.net and Iqaluit Recreational Basketball (Facebook) are adult rec — excluded. Excluded adult-only leagues elsewhere in the territories: Yellowknife Basketball Association, Yellowknife Philippine Basketball League, Yellowknife Women's Basketball, Yukon Men's Basketball League, Whitehorse Women's Basketball League, Filipino Canadian Basketball League of Yukon (runs on TeamLinkt — platform intel) and CFSAY (LeagueLineup).
