verification.claim: Wolf Pack Basketball (Whitehorse club programs + Jr. NBA Yukon) is operated by Wolf Pack Basketball, an independent youth club listed as a youth program partner by Basketball Yukon; only Yukon-wide youth club; no published enrollment numbers; summer programs CA$250; software Squarespace native forms/checkout, no RAMP/TeamLinkt/SportsEngine
verification.evidence: Basketball Yukon lists Wolf Pack under Youth Programs ('Wolf Pack has many programs designed for Yukon youth aged 5-18') — https://basketballyukon.ca/youth-programs/. Club site https://www.wolfpack-basketball.com/about (ages 5-18, Whitehorse + rural Yukon). Four 2026 summer programs each priced CA$250.00 on https://www.wolfpack-basketball.com/programs (13U Boys, 14U HP Boys, 16U Boys HP, 16U Girls). Squarespace confirmed by direct HTML/server fingerprint (curl: 'SQUARESPACE' markers) with on-site sign-up pages (/programs-signup/u-13, /programs-signup/u-17); no RAMP/TeamLinkt/SportsEngine/TeamSnap markers found. Yukon-wide reach corroborated: Jr. NBA delivery in Whitehorse plus Pelly Crossing and Dawson City (https://www.wolfpack-basketball.com/jr-nba); independent coverage: Whitehorse Star 'Wolfpack has success at b-ball tourney' https://www.whitehorsestar.com/Sports/wolfpack-has-success-at-b-ball-tourney.
verification.correction: 'No published enrollment numbers' is slightly off: the club's own public description (mirrored at https://www.findglocal.com/CA/Whitehorse/284432299002453/Wolf-Pack-Basketball) states 'over 100 children and youth ranging in age from 6-18' participate — use ~100+ as a scale estimate; the current /about page itself publishes no figure.

verification.claim: Lynx Basketball (girls development program) is operated by a volunteer-run girls program listed under Basketball Yukon youth programs; scale unknown; software unknown
verification.evidence: Listed under Basketball Yukon Youth Programs as a girls program ('providing girls in Yukon with an opportunity to learn the sport... competitiveness, camaraderie and training') — https://basketballyukon.ca/youth-programs/. Existence independently corroborated by Facebook https://www.facebook.com/lynxbballyukon/, Instagram https://www.instagram.com/lynxbasketballclub/ ('Lynx Basketball Club', Whitehorse YT, girls skills training), and Yukon News coverage of a Whitehorse Lynx U13 girls team https://www.yukon-news.com/sports/whitehorse-lynx-pick-up-wins-in-skagway/. No registration platform detected anywhere; contacts are personal emails/phone (Diedre Davidson, Sarah Crane), consistent with an informal operation.
verification.correction: The specific descriptor 'volunteer-run' is not stated by any source found (Basketball Yukon's page does not say it); it is inferred from personal-gmail contacts. Treat 'volunteer-run' as unverified; everything else stands.

verification.claim: Yukon Lights Out Invitational is operated by Basketball Yukon co-presented with Aasman Brand Communications; 2026 (9th edition) ~370 athletes expected; 2025: 290 players, 30 teams, ~75 games over 4 days; software not identified (Basketball Yukon site WordPress/Divi, no public registration platform)
verification.evidence: Independent: Yukon News (2026-01-29) — 'Basketball Yukon and Aasman Brand Communications will co-present the tournament'; expected 370 athletes; ninth year; 'Last year's event drew 290 players across 30 teams and featured roughly 75 games over four days' — https://yukon-news.com/2026/01/29/yukon-lights-out-basketball-tournament-returns-with-370-players/. Tournament site confirms '9th annual' — https://yukonlightsout.com/. basketballyukon.ca fingerprint: wp-content/themes/Divi (WordPress/Divi) confirmed via curl. yukonlightsout.com/register/ shows no external registration platform (no RAMP/TeamLinkt/SportsEngine/Eventbrite/Google Forms markers).
verification.correction: Two refinements: (1) the tournament's own site foregrounds 'Aasman Brand Communications presents' with Basketball Yukon appearing as a sponsor — co-presentation is the news-corroborated framing, but Aasman is the lead presenter; (2) the tournament runs its own separate site yukonlightsout.com = WordPress + Elementor + SportsPress Pro 2.7 (curl fingerprint), with an on-site register page — better software intel than 'not identified'.

verification.claim: Jr. NBA Yellowknife youth league (formerly Steve Nash Youth Basketball) is operated by Basketball NWT with Canada Basketball's Jr. NBA program; fee $120 + BNWT membership ($150 non-returning); software RAMP InterActive currently, historically GOALLINE (bnwt.goalline.ca dead); stale SportsEngine org page exists
verification.evidence: BNWT Facebook: Jr. NBA Yellowknife (JH Sissons/Joseph school) '$120 + BNWT membership' for returning, '$150 for non-returning' — https://www.facebook.com/bnwt.ca/posts/the-jr-nba-program-in-yellowknife-is-open-for-registrations-this-program-is-for-/1024608016126620/. Independent: CFMWS Yellowknife recreation directory lists the Steve Nash Youth Basketball program under BNWT contact (bnwttd1@gmail.com) — https://cfmws.ca/yellowknife/basketball (SNYB was Canada Basketball's grassroots program rebranded Jr. NBA). Software verified directly: bnwt.ca HTML contains 'rampinteractive' (RAMP InterActive); http://bnwt.goalline.ca/ now redirects to https://stacksports.com/ (dead GOALLINE = Stack Sports); legacy bnwt.ca/page.php GOALLINE URLs return HTTP 500; stale SportsEngine directory page exists at https://www.sportsengine.com/org/basketball-nwt.

verification.claim: YK Youth Basketball League (YYBL) is operated by Basketball NWT; 4 teams in the 2024 season (Lakers beat Thunder in final); was hosted on BNWT's GOALLINE site (bnwt.ca/page.php pages now offline after RAMP migration); current status unclear
verification.evidence: NNSL/Yellowknifer: 'Two points decides Yellowknife Youth Basketball League final' — 2024 season, four teams playing 3-on-3 (grades 7-10, ~5 players each), Lakers beat Thunder 11-9 at William McDonald School — https://www.nnsl.com/sports/two-points-decides-yellowknife-youth-basketball-league-final-7315170. BNWT operation corroborated by BNWT's own Facebook YYBL registration posts (https://www.facebook.com/bnwt.ca/posts/yk-youth-basketball-league-yybl-jr-nba-updatesschool-facilities-are-currently-un/3300848866618505/) and the legacy GOALLINE page http://bnwt.ca/page.php?page_id=124832, which I verified now returns HTTP 500 (offline post-RAMP migration).
verification.correction: 'Current status unclear' can be upgraded: BNWT announced 'Registration Now Open for Jr. NBA & YYBL Spring Seasons' in April 2025 (https://www.instagram.com/p/DIUhfj6Bir8/ and https://www.facebook.com/bnwt.ca/posts/-registration-now-open-for-jr-nba-yybl-spring-seasons-get-ready-for-an-exciting-/1171372271450193/) — YYBL was active as of spring 2025, with registration presumably via BNWT's RAMP site.

verification.claim: GO Ball (girls-only basketball club) is operated by GO Ball, community girls club partnered with Basketball NWT and sponsored by McLennan Ross LLP; every participant gets team gear; alumni feed the Eagles HP program; software Squarespace (goballyk.com); participants must also register with Basketball NWT (RAMP) for 2025-26
verification.evidence: goballyk.com: 'GO Ball is a youth basketball program for girls' in Yellowknife — http://www.goballyk.com/. Independent corroboration of partnership/sponsorship: BNWT's own (indexed) GO Ball listing describes it as 'a girl's only basketball club, partnered with Basketball NWT and sponsored by McLennan Ross LLP' (http://bnwt.ca/page.php?page_id=124830, legacy page) and CFMWS directory lists GO Ball as a BNWT-partnered girls-only program (K-Grade 8) — https://cfmws.ca/yellowknife/basketball. BNWT registration requirement confirmed on http://www.goballyk.com/programs: 'Any new registrants will need to ensure they have registered with BNWT for the 2025-26 season.' Squarespace confirmed twice: squarespace-cdn asset URLs in page content and curl server fingerprint. Eagles pathway supported: GO Ball's own news post announced the Eagles club for Grades 6-12 (http://www.goballyk.com/news/2020/10/1/new-eagles-basketball-club-grades-6-12) and the site has a 'GOBall Graduates' section.
verification.correction: Two soft spots: (1) 'every participant gets team gear' could not be verified on any GO Ball page (programs/register make no gear mention) — treat as unverified; (2) the BNWT/RAMP registration requirement is stated for NEW registrants specifically. Current divisions listed are U6-U10 (spring), with the broader program historically K-Grade 8.

verification.claim: Yellowknife Eagles Basketball is a girls high-performance club affiliated with Basketball NWT, successor to the early-90s YK High Performance Girls program; multiple age-group teams; produces post-secondary athletes; software RAMP Registrations + Google Forms
verification.evidence: Club site https://www.eaglesball.ca/ — girls HP program, three teams (U13/U15/U19, Grades 6-12), BNWT logo displayed, and homepage registration via both RAMP Registrations (rampregistrations.com) and a Google Forms link. History independently corroborated: Yellowknifer 'Protect the nest: new Eagles girls basketball program planned for Yellowknife' (Sept 2020, merger of U19 Deton Cho Eagles + U13/U15 Diamonds) — https://yellowknifer.com/2020/09/15/protect-the-nest-new-eagles-girls-basketball-program-planned-for-yellowknife/ — and NNSL season coverage https://www.nnsl.com/sports/yellowknife-eagles-girls-basketball-program-gets-ready-for-2021-22-season/. Legacy BNWT listing 'Eagles Basketball (Girls HP)' at http://bnwt.ca/page.php?page_id=124834 confirms BNWT affiliation. Early-90s HP-program lineage is stated in the club's own About text; 'produces post-secondary athletes' is the club's claim (no independent roster-level verification, but nothing contradicts).
verification.correction: Additional software intel: eaglesball.ca itself is built on Wix (generator meta tag 'Wix.com Website Builder'), with RAMP + Google Forms used only for registration.

verification.claim: Yellowknife Ravens Basketball Club (boys) is a boys club and sister program to the Eagles; 3 age groups; software RAMP Registrations (RavensBasketballClub.rampregistrations.com)
verification.evidence: Club site https://www.ykravensbasketball.com/ — boys program, explicitly cross-links 'our Sister Program: Eagles Basketball Program' for female athletes; registration link http://RavensBasketballClub.rampregistrations.com verified in page HTML (curl fingerprint 'rampregistrations.com'); 2025-26 season Oct-May with U13/U14/U16 registration open. Legacy BNWT listing 'Ravens Basketball (Boys HP)' at http://bnwt.ca/page.php?page_id=124835 corroborates the boys-HP affiliation.
verification.correction: Scale should be 4 age groups, not 3: the club's Our Coaches page (https://www.ykravensbasketball.com/our-coaches) lists coached teams at U13, U14, U16 AND U18 (HC Mark Matheson). Registration is currently open for 3 of the 4 (U13/U14/U16).

verification.claim: Club Wolf Pack Basketball (Whitehorse) exists and its teams play in Jr. NBA Yukon, Yukon Lights Out Invitational youth divisions, and out-of-territory tournaments in BC/Alberta/Alaska
verification.evidence: Existence: https://www.wolfpack-basketball.com/about + Basketball Yukon listing https://basketballyukon.ca/youth-programs/. Jr. NBA Yukon: club delivers official Jr. NBA programs with Canada Basketball in Whitehorse, Pelly Crossing and Dawson City — https://www.wolfpack-basketball.com/jr-nba. Out-of-territory BC/AB/AK: U-17 HP program 'plays exhibition games in Alaska and participates in tournaments... in Alberta and British Columbia' — https://www.wolfpack-basketball.com/programs-signup/u-17; independently, Whitehorse Star reports Wolfpack took three teams to Calgary's Stampede Showdown and all won their divisions — https://www.whitehorsestar.com/Sports/wolfpack-has-success-at-b-ball-tourney.
verification.correction: The Lights Out entry is unverified: no source found showing Wolf Pack teams entering the Yukon Lights Out Invitational (2026 youth divisions are boys/girls 15-18 plus a Grade 8 bracket; no Wolf Pack rosters appear in tournament coverage at https://yukonlightsout.com/results/ or news reports). Downgrade 'plays in Lights Out youth divisions' to unconfirmed/possible.

verification.claim: Club Lynx Basketball (Lynx Basketball Club, Whitehorse) exists; leagues it plays in unknown
verification.evidence: Existence corroborated by three independent sources: Basketball Yukon youth-programs listing (https://basketballyukon.ca/youth-programs/), the club's Facebook (https://www.facebook.com/lynxbballyukon/) and Instagram (https://www.instagram.com/lynxbasketballclub/), plus Yukon News coverage — https://www.yukon-news.com/sports/whitehorse-lynx-pick-up-wins-in-skagway/.
verification.correction: Can enrich the 'plays in ?' field: a Whitehorse Lynx U13 girls team (grades 5-8, multi-school) competed at the Boyd Worley Elementary Basketball Tournament in Skagway, Alaska (Yukon News, Nov 2013) — historical evidence of out-of-territory Alaska play; no current league affiliation found.

verification.claim: Basketball Yukon (Whitehorse) exists; involved in Yukon Lights Out Invitational (co-presenter with Aasman) and Jr. NBA Yukon (delivered via Wolf Pack)
verification.evidence: PSO exists at https://basketballyukon.ca/ (WordPress/Divi, verified). Lights Out co-presentation: Yukon News — 'Basketball Yukon and Aasman Brand Communications will co-present the tournament' — https://yukon-news.com/2026/01/29/yukon-lights-out-basketball-tournament-returns-with-370-players/. Jr. NBA delivered via Wolf Pack: Wolf Pack's Jr. NBA page (https://www.wolfpack-basketball.com/jr-nba, official Canada Basketball Jr. NBA programs in Yukon) and Basketball Yukon's own site hosting Wolf Pack program pages (https://basketballyukon.ca/wolf-pack-fall-development-series/ and https://basketballyukon.ca/youth-programs/). Note for the data model: Basketball Yukon is the territorial governing body (PSO), not a club fielding teams; and the tournament's own site bills Aasman as lead presenter with Basketball Yukon as sponsor/co-presenter.

verification.claim: Basketball NWT (Yellowknife) exists; runs YK Youth Basketball League (YYBL), Jr. NBA Yellowknife youth league (formerly SNYB), and the U14 Futures Jr. High Performance Program
verification.evidence: PSO exists: https://bnwt.ca/ live on RAMP InterActive (verified by HTML fingerprint), plus SportsEngine directory page https://www.sportsengine.com/org/basketball-nwt and Facebook https://www.facebook.com/bnwt.ca/. YYBL: BNWT Facebook registration posts + NNSL final coverage (https://www.nnsl.com/sports/two-points-decides-yellowknife-youth-basketball-league-final-7315170). Jr. NBA: BNWT Facebook fee post (https://www.facebook.com/bnwt.ca/posts/the-jr-nba-program-in-yellowknife-is-open-for-registrations-this-program-is-for-/1024608016126620/). U14 Futures: independent CFMWS directory lists 'U14 Futures Jr. High Performance Boys Program' run by Basketball NWT (Grades 6-8, boys) — https://cfmws.ca/yellowknife/basketball; Ravens site notes the program is in its 4th year; Instagram https://www.instagram.com/futuresbasketballnwt/. Note: BNWT is the territorial PSO, not a club.

verification.claim: Yellowknife Eagles Basketball exists and its teams play in out-of-territory tournaments (e.g., Volvo of Edmonton Challenge)
verification.evidence: BallerTV streamed 'Yellowknife Eagles 17U Girls vs. Supreme Hoops U17 Girls' at the Swoosh Volvo of Edmonton Challenge (2022) — https://www.ballertv.com/streams/yellowknife-eagles-17u-girls-vs-supreme-hoops-u17-girls-20220521021243-kxtqzmbq. NNSL (May 2023): Eagles U19 won the U17 girls silver division at the Swoosh Volvo of Edmonton Challenge, with U13/U15 Eagles also travelling (U15 beat Win Basketball of Calgary 40-38 OT) — https://www.nnsl.com/sports/a-winning-weekend-on-the-basketball-court-for-yellowknife-eagles-and-winmar-spartans-7273864. Tournament itself: https://www.swooshcanadatournaments.com/volvo-of-edmonton-challenge.

verification.claim: Yellowknife Ravens Basketball Club exists and its teams play in out-of-territory tournaments
verification.evidence: Existence confirmed (https://www.ykravensbasketball.com/ + legacy BNWT listing http://bnwt.ca/page.php?page_id=124835). But no source found showing teams under the RAVENS name at an out-of-territory tournament. Strong indirect support: the Yellowknife boys HP program — which the Ravens site says 'has evolved with different names' since the early 90s — competed as the Winmar Spartans at the Swoosh Volvo of Edmonton Challenge in May 2023, winning the U17 boys bronze division (https://www.nnsl.com/sports/a-winning-weekend-on-the-basketball-court-for-yellowknife-eagles-and-winmar-spartans-7273864), and the Ravens site celebrates Team NT results. Nothing contradicts the claim; keep as plausible until a Ravens-branded tournament entry is found.
